--- KickstartAI ---

belief: AI should not only be approached as a machine for working more efficiently but also as a means to create societal impact and support people’s needs.
source_quote: “If we approach AI only as a machine for working more efficiently, we are selling ourselves short.”
source_text:
The questions Dusée encounters within ING are by no means unique to banks, he says. That is precisely why he is active within KickstartAI, the partnership involving organizations including KLM, NS, ING and Ahold Delhaize, which aims to accelerate AI adoption in the Netherlands. The sectors may differ, but the underlying AI challenges are often much more similar.

“KLM’s market is completely different from ING’s,” he says. “But you encounter the same challenges. From governance and data to the question: how do you move from a proof of concept to a real product?” For organizations, he believes, the value is immediate: learning faster what works and what does not.

For Dusée, KickstartAI also has a broader meaning than knowledge exchange between large companies. He explicitly sees it as a way to create more societal impact. As an example, he mentions an AI tool around financial health that ING is currently exploring  together with KickstartAI, with the aim of eventually investigating how customers can be helped earlier and more accessibly when financial problems are at risk of emerging.

Dusée: “How do you reach people earlier, lower barriers and offer help in a way that matches what people need? I find that at least as relevant. If we approach AI only as a machine for working more efficiently, we are selling ourselves short.”

But for Dusée, the value of KickstartAI extends beyond the partners alone. He sees the network not only as a place where large organizations exchange knowledge, but also as a way to make expertise more broadly available.

“There are so many organizations in the Netherlands, both profit and non-profit, where AI can be incredibly valuable,” he says. “In education, research, and healthcare, for example. But not everyone has the financial resources or capacity to apply AI quickly. How valuable is it, then, if we can contribute through KickstartAI and make our knowledge available?”

That is why he hopes more organizations will join. Not because every partner has to do the same thing, but because a larger network, in his view, makes more possible: more exchange, more learning capacity and ultimately more societal impact.

Dusée: “Those who work together not only increase the chances of scaling within their own organization, but also of creating real societal impact.”

--- Five lessons from Leon Dusée for other organizations ---

1. Choose sharply, and consciously leave things aside

According to Dusée, the biggest mistake is not experimenting too little, but wanting to do too much at once. Leaders must dare to say: this is where we will focus, and not there for now. Without focus, AI remains fragmented.

2. Look beyond the individual use case to the entire process

Many ideas rightly emerge bottom-up, but remain too small. The leadership question, he says, is always: can you broaden an application from one report or task to the entire process? That is where real scale lies.

3. Think about day two from day one

Pilots often stall because ownership, maintenance and embedding in existing processes are considered too late. Do not optimize for the demo, but for practice.

4. Invest as much in people as in technology

AI raises the demands placed on employees. Subject-matter expertise, adaptability and the ability to challenge output become more important, not less. Dusée also explicitly mentions learning on the job and a different perspective on education.

5. See AI as societal infrastructure too

According to Dusée, AI should not only be about productivity. Especially in sectors such as education, research and healthcare, collaboration can help make applications more accessible to organizations that do not themselves have the resources to lead the way.

Thank you for subscribing to our newsletter!

Many ideas rightly emerge bottom-up, but remain too small. The leadership question, he says, is always: can you broaden an application from one report or task to the entire process? That is where real scale lies.

3. Think about day two from day one

Pilots often stall because ownership, maintenance and embedding in existing processes are considered too late. Do not optimize for the demo, but for practice.

4. Invest as much in people as in technology

AI raises the demands placed on employees. Subject-matter expertise, adaptability and the ability to challenge output become more important, not less. Dusée also explicitly mentions learning on the job and a different perspective on education.

5. See AI as societal infrastructure too

According to Dusée, AI should not only be about productivity. Especially in sectors such as education, research and healthcare, collaboration can help make applications more accessible to organizations that do not themselves have the resources to lead the way.

💡 Want to learn more about the KickstartAI community and our mission to accelerate AI adoption in the Netherlands? Check out our website , listen to our podcast , or follow us on LinkedIn !

Thank you for subscribing to our newsletter!

--- AI lessons from the boardroom ---

=== KLM CIO Aart Slagt on why an AI model only has value if users understand and trust it ===

AI is now on almost every agenda. Not as a gadget, but as a strategic question: what should you use it for, how do you scale it, and how do you prevent experiments from getting stuck in the pilot phase? In the interview series "AI lessons from the boardroom," we speak with executives and board members who have to make those choices in practice. In this edition: Aart Slagt, Executive Vice President Information Services & CIO at KLM Royal Dutch Airlines. “The time for experimentation alone is over. We are now focused on truly implementing AI at the core of our business.”

Aart Slagt has already had a full day by the time he sits down to talk about artificial intelligence. Meetings, coordination, decisions, operational questions: as CIO of KLM, he constantly moves between technology and practice. That also fits his profile. Slagt is not an executive of fashionable buzzwords, but someone who comes from operations and still looks at things through that lens. After ten years in operational roles and now five years as CIO, he knows that plans fall apart if they do not resonate with colleagues on the operational side.

“We, too, have gone through a phase of experimentation here.” It’s been a useful stage, says Slagt, “because it helped the business and IT understand each other better.” But as far as he is concerned, that phase is now over. “We can no longer let a thousand flowers bloom. We want to create focus: where does KLM want to deploy AI to the fullest, which processes does the company want to redesign from an AI-first perspective, and where do we deliberately choose not to apply it?”

Not every part of KLM lends itself to far-reaching automation, Slagt knows. Some things you simply want people to do. “Take cabin service on our aircraft. That cannot exist without human contact.” But in other areas, AI can make a real difference, and that is exactly where you want to accelerate it: in planning, optimization, administration, and support. A good example is the model KLM built together with KickstartAI to better predict how much catering is needed on board , based on the actual number of passengers who end up taking the flight. It sounds small, but the impact is significant. “On the one hand, you can serve passengers better with the catering that is really needed,” says Slagt. “On the other hand, you prevent a huge amount of food waste, because far less has to be thrown away. So it is a double win.”

--- Trust and explainability ---

Another example Slagt is proud of concerns a joint project on contingency fuel : the additional fuel an aircraft carries for emergencies and unexpected circumstances. It naturally requires a delicate balance. Safety always comes first, but carrying too much fuel is also unsustainable and costly. “If you need it, it has to be on board. But you also do not want to take too much, because it adds unnecessary weight.”

Together with KickstartAI, KLM built a model that takes a higher number of variables into account than the traditional approach. Think of load, passenger numbers, wind, weather systems, and conditions at the destination. It is a unique model, but “ultimately, the captain is responsible and decides how much fuel is taken on board. So it is important that you can explain how such a model works, which variables go into it, and why the advice comes out the way it does.”

--- Knowledge ---

belief: KLM should create focus by deciding where to deploy AI to the fullest, which processes to redesign from an AI-first perspective, and where to deliberately choose not to apply it.

--- AI lessons from the boardroom ---

=== KLM CIO Aart Slagt on why an AI model only has value if users understand and trust it ===

AI is now on almost every agenda. Not as a gadget, but as a strategic question: what should you use it for, how do you scale it, and how do you prevent experiments from getting stuck in the pilot phase? In this edition: Aart Slagt, Executive Vice President Information Services & CIO at KLM Royal Dutch Airlines. “The time for experimentation alone is over. It sounds small, but the impact is significant. “On the one hand, you can serve passengers better with the catering that is really needed,” says Slagt. “On the other hand, you prevent a huge amount of food waste, because far less has to be thrown away. So it is a double win.”

--- Trust and explainability ---

Another example Slagt is proud of concerns a joint project on contingency fuel : the additional fuel an aircraft carries for emergencies and unexpected circumstances. It naturally requires a delicate balance. Safety always comes first, but carrying too much fuel is also unsustainable and costly. “If you need it, it has to be on board. But you also do not want to take too much, because it adds unnecessary weight.”

Together with KickstartAI, KLM built a model that takes a higher number of variables into account than the traditional approach. Think of load, passenger numbers, wind, weather systems, and conditions at the destination. It is a unique model, but “ultimately, the captain is responsible and decides how much fuel is taken on board. So it is important that you can explain how such a model works, which variables go into it, and why the advice comes out the way it does.”

--- Knowledge ---

belief: End users and executives must deeply understand AI’s capabilities and limitations for AI initiatives to create real change in practice.
source_quote: “If you have to make decisions about investments, risks, and applications, you cannot follow this from a distance. You really have to immerse yourself in what AI can and cannot do.”
source_text:
And that, according to Slagt, is exactly where things often go wrong. “A model may look excellent on paper, but if people do not understand it, nothing changes on the work floor.” That is precisely why it is important for end users (in this case pilots as well as the pilots’ union,) to be involved early in the development of such an application. And it is equally important for executives to understand both the capabilities and the limitations of AI. Slagt says: “If you have to make decisions about investments, risks, and applications, you cannot follow this from a distance. You really have to immerse yourself in what AI can and cannot do.”

At KLM, that meant the leadership team did not get yet another presentation, but instead had to sit down behind a laptop themselves, in this case at Google’s office. Slagt: “The assignment was simple: build your own AI assistant for an advertising campaign. Or create an agent that scans aviation news every week and puts it in your inbox. Only when you do it yourself do you see what AI can  deliver.”

KLM noticed the same thing when generative AI started advancing rapidly and employees wanted to start working with it. That is why KLM built Talia: a secure environment in which employees can safely work with models such as Gemini, Mistral, and ChatGPT. “It is important to keep building the bridge between the theory behind AI and its practical application. The colleagues that will work with it need to understand it, embrace it, and gain trust in it.”

--- The Dutch debate ---

But according to Slagt, the learning doesn't stop there. Anyone who really wants to make serious progress with AI also has to look outside their company.For that reason, KLM is active in KickstartAI, an initiative it co-founded with NS, Ahold, and ING. For Slagt, the value lies not only in the technology, but also in the way of collaborating: companies, sectors, and specialists coming together to solve real problems and put AI on the map in the Netherlands.

He calls KickstartAI, not without reason, “the coalition of the doing.” It is not a network of endless meetings and discussion groups, but a foundation that simply gets to work and delivers strong results.

And according to Slagt, that is exactly where the gain lies. “By joining forces with other companies, you bring in AI knowledge as well as  a fresh perspective on your own processes. It is precisely that combination of data and AI specialists with people who sometimes come from completely different sectors that produces the best solutions. I would like to see more companies in the Netherlands benefit from that by joining KickstartAI.”

It is a pragmatic and open perspective that is still far from self-evident in the Netherlands, Slagt observes. “ We sometimes focus too much on the harm AI could cause,” he says. “And then we surround the technology with fences, rules, and restrictions.”

A shame, Slagt believes. Because AI can make companies more productive, organize processes more intelligently, and strengthen the Netherlands as a knowledge economy. At the same time, international developments are not standing still. Other countries are investing heavily in AI and rapidly building up knowledge and applications. “If we do not do it, someone else will,” he concludes. “Then we will be left behind. It would be a great shame if we let that happen.”

--- Three lessons from Aart Slagt for other organizations ---

1. Stop open-ended experimentation in time Pilots and proof-of-concepts are necessary to explore possibilities and bring business and IT closer together. But at a certain point, an organization has to make choices. Where do you really want to deploy AI, what do you want to scale up, and where do you deliberately choose not to apply the technology?

source_text:
AI is high on every agenda. But for many organizations, the question is where to start – and how to move beyond isolated initiatives.

In our latest article, we speak with Leon Dusée, Global Head COO Risk at ING about how to apply AI in practice. He explains that successful AI adoption requires focus, clear choices, and early thinking about how solutions will scale within the organization.

In the interview, Dusée shares lessons on:
🔸 Why AI pilots often stall
🔸 What leaders need to take into account when implementing AI solutions
🔸 The importance of investing in people alongside technology
🔸 And how AI can contribute to broader societal impact

belief: There is an urgent need to move from discussing AI to executing and applying it in practice.
source_quote: the urgency is there – now execution needs to follow
source_text:
Last Thursday, our CEO Erick Webbe joined the roundtable discussion in the Dutch Parliament (Tweede Kamer der Staten-Generaal) on how the Netherlands can make better use of the opportunities AI offers. 🇳🇱 

Our view is clear: the urgency is there – now execution needs to follow.
As Erick put it: "We need to take AI out of the ivory tower and into practice.”

That broader message was reflected in several parts of the discussion. As Maarten de Rijke also noted, the Netherlands has spent a long time thinking through the rules around AI; now it is time to start playing.

The Netherlands already has a strong foundation – from long-term research through ELLIS - European Laboratory for Learning and Intelligent Systems, to mid-term collaboration through ICAI - Innovation Center for Artificial Intelligence labs, to short-cycle implementation through initiatives like KickstartAI. What is still missing is not ideas, talent, or infrastructure, but turning that capacity into actual use.

The next step is to build on what already works. As @Petra Piersma noted, that means focusing on scaling proven solutions. 📊 

That’s the part we focus on at KickstartAI: working with organisations to apply AI in practice and sharing the learnings so they can be replicated across the Dutch ecosystem. 

We’d be happy to welcome interested MPs Claire Martens - America, Henk Vermeer, Ouafa Oualhadj, Jantine Zwinkels, Sarah El Boujdaini, Barbara Kathmann to one of our upcoming project launches and show what that real life AI solutions look like in action. 🚀  

#VVD #CDA #BBB #D66 #PRO #KickstartAI #AI

belief: The next step for the Netherlands is to focus on scaling proven AI solutions that already work.
source_quote: that means focusing on scaling proven solutions
source_text:
Last Thursday, our CEO Erick Webbe joined the roundtable discussion in the Dutch Parliament (Tweede Kamer der Staten-Generaal) on how the Netherlands can make better use of the opportunities AI offers. 🇳🇱 

Our view is clear: the urgency is there – now execution needs to follow.
As Erick put it: "We need to take AI out of the ivory tower and into practice.”

That broader message was reflected in several parts of the discussion. As Maarten de Rijke also noted, the Netherlands has spent a long time thinking through the rules around AI; now it is time to start playing.

The Netherlands already has a strong foundation – from long-term research through ELLIS - European Laboratory for Learning and Intelligent Systems, to mid-term collaboration through ICAI - Innovation Center for Artificial Intelligence labs, to short-cycle implementation through initiatives like KickstartAI. What is still missing is not ideas, talent, or infrastructure, but turning that capacity into actual use.

The next step is to build on what already works. As @Petra Piersma noted, that means focusing on scaling proven solutions. 📊 

That’s the part we focus on at KickstartAI: working with organisations to apply AI in practice and sharing the learnings so they can be replicated across the Dutch ecosystem. 

We’d be happy to welcome interested MPs Claire Martens - America, Henk Vermeer, Ouafa Oualhadj, Jantine Zwinkels, Sarah El Boujdaini, Barbara Kathmann to one of our upcoming project launches and show what that real life AI solutions look like in action. 🚀  

#VVD #CDA #BBB #D66 #PRO #KickstartAI #AI

belief: Work with AI is more rewarding when it has a broader societal purpose.
source_quote: even more rewarding when there’s a broader societal purpose behind her work
source_text:
🚀 Meet Anouk Wolters, AI Project Lead at KickstartAI!

Anouk works at the intersection of technology, people, and implementation – helping turn AI ideas into concrete solutions.

What drives her the most? Making sure AI genuinely serves the people who end up using it. That becomes even more rewarding when there’s a broader societal purpose behind her work – like in a project she’s currently leading at Leiden University Medical Center, where we're developing an AI solution that reduces nurses’ workload while improving wound care.

👉 Read her full story here: https://lnkd.in/em9vdXYh

belief: Organisations should not have to work through new developments in isolation but should build on each other’s experiences.
source_quote: so organisations don't have to work through these developments in isolation, but can build on each other’s experiences
source_text:
🚀 This month, we kicked off the first of our new Knowledge Sharing Sessions with our corporate partners: Ahold Delhaize, ING, KLM Royal Dutch Airlines, and Nederlandse Spoorwegen.

These closed-door sessions are designed to create space to share what teams are seeing, testing, and learning – so organisations don't have to work through these developments in isolation, but can build on each other’s experiences.

For this first edition, we focused on MCPs (Model Context Protocol) and AI agents, opening with a keynote by Bob van Luijt. We moved into roundtable discussions on where these building blocks are already showing up, what teams are experimenting with, and where the practical questions still are.

The goal is simple: by turning these insights into a shared knowledge base, the whole group can move forward faster. A big thank you to all the participants! 👏 

#AIAgents #MCPs #KickstartAI #AppliedAI

belief: The Netherlands has many prerequisites in place for AI, such as talent, infrastructure, and a strong business climate, but is losing momentum due to low AI implementation capacity and outdated structures.
source_quote: our position is clear: The Netherlands 🇳🇱  has many prerequisites in place – talent, infrastructure, and a strong business climate – but we are losing momentum to low AI implementation capacity and structures that weren't built for the speed of this technology.
source_text:
We are proud to share that our CEO, Erick Webbe, will be taking part in a roundtable discussion at the Dutch Parliament (Tweede Kamer der Staten-Generaal) on 26 March about optimizing AI opportunities for the Netherlands.

At KickstartAI, our position is clear: The Netherlands 🇳🇱  has many prerequisites in place – talent, infrastructure, and a strong business climate – but we are losing momentum to low AI implementation capacity and structures that weren't built for the speed of this technology. As a result, we're not realizing the full potential of AI and its benefits for society.

Erick will be advocating for a national shift from talk to action. To do that, we need:
✅ Space for execution and a mandate for practical experimentation. 
✅ Moving past existing structures to prioritize performance and scale. 
✅ Bridging the impact gap by tackling today’s most complex challenges

We know this is possible through our own projects – from reducing food waste with Ahold Delhaize, to optimizing logistics with Ampère, and improving financial health with ING.

📖 You can find our full position paper on the Parliament’s website here: https://lnkd.in/e2X6exwK 

Looking forward to the conversation Claire Martens - America, Judith Bühler, Ouafa Oualhadj, Barbara Kathmann, Tom van der Lee, Daniël van den Berg!

belief: Starting AI implementation with the technology instead of the problem is a common trap for organizations.
source_quote: a common trap in AI implementation: starting with the technology instead of the problem
source_text:
In an interview published this week, Erick Webbe, our CEO, points out a common trap in AI implementation: starting with the technology instead of the problem.

“We want to do something with AI” is still where many organizations begin. But without a clear problem to solve, that’s usually where things start to stall.

As AI becomes a “system technology”– much like electricity or the internet – what does deliberate application look like and what kind of challenges do organizations need to solve?

👉 Read the full interview here: https://lnkd.in/ewkX_w6Z

belief: AI tools should be used to help investigators navigate complex legislation, case law, and similar cases more quickly in environmental crime investigations.
source_quote: an AI tool that helps investigators navigate legislation, case law and similar cases more quickly, supporting the early analysis phase of environmental crime investigations
source_text:
Environmental crime investigations are some of the most complex cases police deal with, multiple layers of legislation.

This is why, together with Politie Nederland, KickstartAI is working on an AI tool that helps investigators navigate legislation, case law and similar cases more quickly, supporting the early analysis phase of environmental crime investigations.

We spoke with Amir Niknam, senior innovation advisor at the Police, about the project and the role AI can play in supporting investigators. 

🎓 His interview also kicks off our new series "AI Lessons from the Boardroom", where we speak with executives about how they approach the governance and use of AI in their organizations.

👇 Read the full article below: 
https://lnkd.in/euaR2tME

#AI #EnvironmentalCrime #AILessonsFromTheBoardroom

belief: AI products should be designed starting from human psychology, workflows, and trust.
source_quote: Start with human psychology, workflows, and trust.
source_text:
🚀 LastFriday, we had a full house at our KickstartAI x Data Science Rotterdam meetup on scaling AI products.

We heard valuable insights from founders navigating this journey every day:
🔸 Leen de Graaf (Cuurios) on building targeted AI solutions instead of one-size-fits-all products
 🔸 Guus van Heijningen (Captain) on raising funding for an AI startup
 🔸 Sohrab Hosseini (orq.ai) and Karthik Mahadevan (Envision) on the realities of scaling 

💡 Key takeaways:
 • Start with human psychology, workflows, and trust. 
 • Think like an investor when building your pitch.
 • Build internationally from day one.

Thanks to our speakers, the evening's moderator Michael Niemantsverdriet, and everyone who joined the discussion.

Save the date for our upcoming meetup on April 2!
#AIMeetup  #ScalingAI

belief: Data composition and data quality are just as crucial to progress in language models as bigger architectures and larger datasets.
source_quote: data composition and data quality are just as crucial
source_text:
We often credit progress in language models to bigger architectures and ever-larger datasets. But questions of data composition and data quality are just as crucial – and far less understood.

📖 In our latest article, we spoke with Dong Nguyen (Associate Professor at Utrecht University) about DataDivers, an ongoing research project on how to measure data diversity in language model datasets – and how this can help us better understand model behavior and performance.

Read the full article below 👇 
https://lnkd.in/e9CD-i8j

#AI #DataDiversity #LLMs #NLP

belief: Applying AI in the Netherlands requires a sense of urgency and implementation of the coalition agreement commitments.
source_quote: Het toepassen van AI in Nederland vraagt om urgentie én om het uitvoeren van de afspraken uit het coalitieakkoord.
source_text:
Hartelijk dank voor jullie bezoek, Ouafa Oualhadj, Sarah El Boujdaini en Fabian Snijders (D66), aan KickstartAI. Waardevol om – juist op de dag dat Jetten I van start ging – samen te brainstormen over hoe we concrete stappen kunnen zetten in AI-adoptie.

💡 Op de agenda:
 🔸 Welke lessen kunnen we leren uit de praktijk van AI-adoptie? Denk aan het oplossen van maatschappelijke vraagstukken, het effectiever maken van de overheid en het versterken van bedrijven met AI.
 🔸 Concrete voorbeelden: we deelden hoe AI nu al aantoonbare waarde toevoegt voor onze partners en de organisaties waarmee we samenwerken.
 🔸 Verantwoorde AI: een open en eerlijke dialoog over wat nodig is voor veilige, ethische én innovatieve AI-toepassingen in Nederland.

Het toepassen van AI in Nederland vraagt om urgentie én om het uitvoeren van de afspraken uit het coalitieakkoord. Nu is het moment om woorden om te zetten in daden. Dat vraagt om durf om actie te nemen – bijvoorbeeld via sterke publiek-private samenwerking.

Veel dank voor jullie komst, jullie betrokkenheid bij AI en – bovenal – de gedeelde ambitie om de kansen van AI in de praktijk te realiseren! 

#AIAdoptie #Innovatie #DigitaleZaken

belief: People considering a career in AI should focus on understanding the fundamentals rather than getting attached to specific tools or techniques.

source_text:
🚀Meet Jorrit van der Laan, ML Engineer at KickstartAI!

With a background in mathematics and data science, Jorrit enjoys solving problems and finding patterns.

What he finds special about the AI field is that deep technical work goes hand in hand with collaborating with people from diverse backgrounds and understanding different perspectives.

💡His advice to people considering a career in AI? “Don’t get too attached to specific tools or techniques. Focus on understanding the fundamentals – they’ll always stay relevant.”

belief: AI can help our society thrive for the benefit of all.
source_quote: a shared belief that AI can help our society thrive for the benefit of all
source_text:
It was a real pleasure being part of last week’s AI4ALL Gala & Awards – an evening of inspiration, celebration, and a shared belief that AI can help our society thrive for the benefit of all.

We were honoured to sponsor the AI4Her Earth Award, recognizing the use of AI to address environmental challenges, and proud to have our CEO Erick Webbe present it to Dr. Rimma Dzhusupova, whose work applies AI to optimize resource-intensive industries – from improving complex engineering processes to supporting offshore green hydrogen production.

Congratulations to Rimma on this well-deserved recognition, to fellow category finalists Anna Brueva and Inge van den Ende, and to all the nominees and winners of the evening. ✨ 

Events like this show why community matters.

If AI is to strengthen our economy and society we have to keep building ecosystems where people collaborate, share knowledge, and turn ideas into action. 

👏 A big thank you to the AI4ALL founders Oliviana Bailey, Karen S. Harriss, MBA, GMA, MA, and Jana Wilhelmus for all their work! 

📸 Credits to Lera Manzovitova for the first two photos. 

#AI #KickstartAI #AI4ALL

belief: Improving customer experience on bol’s platform is an important goal of their AI work.
source_quote: with the ultimate goal to improve customer experience on bol’s platform
source_text:
Exciting news! We’ve completed the KickstartAI x bol Challenge: Optimizing the Delivery Promise 🚚

Last month, 16 engineers from KickstartAI, bol, KLM Royal Dutch Airlines, ING, Ampère, Nederlandse Spoorwegen, and Ahold Delhaize came together for a two-week AI sprint.

💡 The focus was practical: working on a delivery prediction model with the ultimate goal to improve customer experience on bol’s platform. 

Working in mixed teams, participants tested ideas, compared approaches, and built on each other’s work in a short, hands-on setup. A visit to a bol warehouse added valuable context and helped connect the technical work to day-to-day operations. 

The challenge concluded with a closing session and drinks, wrapping up two weeks of close collaboration across organizations. Together with our partners, we believe in sharing knowledge, building collectively, and accelerating AI adoption across the Netherlands 🇳🇱

#AI #MachineLearning #AIinNL #Collaboration

belief: The urgency around labour productivity, national earning capacity, and ensuring a positive societal impact is undeniable and demands action now.
source_quote: The urgency around labour productivity, our national earning capacity, and ensuring a positive societal impact  is undeniable – and it demands action now.
source_text:
On Monday, KickstartAI joined Minister Vincent Karremans and a small group of industry leaders at the Ministry of Economic Affairs for a roundtable on AI adoption in the Netherlands.

🇳🇱 The urgency around labour productivity, our national earning capacity, and ensuring a positive societal impact  is undeniable – and it demands action now. If we want to stay competitive, we need to treat the Netherlands as a scale-up country for AI adoption – moving beyond high-level reflections to the reality of implementation in day-to-day operations.

For the past four years, KickstartAI and our founding partners have been working exactly there: in the core of organizations. Our experience shows that meaningful AI adoption is a steep learning curve. It means tackling real problems, embedding AI into core processes, and dealing with culture and governance – not just rolling out tools.

We believe large companies play a crucial role as the flywheel for Dutch AI innovation and practical adoption at scale. Our new CEO Erick Webbe indicated we are committed to staying the course, with the ambition to build on it nationally and deliver a tenfold increase in lasting impact: with big and small businesses. 

Looking at the new coalition agreement this is a shared goal. The focus now must be on turning it into practice. 

A sincere thank you to Vincent Karremans and Martijn Lucassen for the invitation and to all participants for the open and practical discussion Art de Blaauw, Hiek Van der Scheer, Juliette Goetzee, Saskia Mureau, Thomas Grosfeld, Jelle Prins!

belief: Organisations that believe in the future economy and want to be digital frontrunners must take concrete steps in AI.
source_quote: Wie gelooft in de economie van de toekomst en digitaal koploper wil zijn, zal hier echt stappen in moeten zetten.
source_text:
De ambitie op AI is duidelijk verankerd in het coalitieakkoord ‘Aan de Slag.’ Mooi om te zien dat dit wordt vertaald naar concrete acties: inzet op een NADI, aandacht voor AI-geletterdheid, aanpassing van de WBSO om de ontwikkeling van AI te stimuleren, en uiteraard een verantwoorde inzet van AI binnen de overheid zelf. 

Wie gelooft in de economie van de toekomst en digitaal koploper wil zijn, zal hier echt stappen in moeten zetten.

➡️ Van pilot naar implementatie; van klein beginnen naar opschalen en vooral in de praktijk doen. Bij KickstartAI herkennen we dat: al meer dan vijf jaar werken wij aan het versnellen van AI-adoptie. Wij kijken ernaar uit deze uitdaging samen met Kabinet en Kamer op te pakken.

belief: Progress in AI is not just about technical capability but about making deliberate choices along the way.
source_quote: progress isn’t just about technical capability, but about making deliberate choices along the way
source_text:
Last Friday, the KickstartAI community gathered at the Hortus Botanicus Amsterdam to start 2026 by (re)connecting with each other and discussing our work in AI 🤝 

Through facilitated networking rounds, people quickly got specific about their roles, current challenges, and what kind of collaboration would actually be useful. 

From there, Roel Willems (Ahold Delhaize) offered an update on the state of AI adoption in the Netherlands – a timely reminder that progress isn’t just about technical capability, but about making deliberate choices along the way.

We ended with a guided walk through the gardens, which turned out to be a fitting metaphor: healthy ecosystems depend on diversity, cross-pollination, and long-term thinking – much like the AI ecosystem we’re building. 

🌟 A big thank you to everyone who joined! 

#KickstartAICommunity #AINetherlands #AI

belief: Identifying potential areas for collaboration is a key starting point for forming coalitions that can move faster on strategic initiatives such as shared European infrastructure.
source_quote: a key starting point for identifying coalitions that could move faster on strategic initiatives such as shared European infrastructure
source_text:
Over the past months, we collaborated with DenkWerk on their new report examining Europe’s geopolitical and geo-economic position – and what it will take to strengthen Europe’s ability to act.

💡Thanks to the work of our ML Engineer Cascha van Wanrooij we used AI to transcribe and analyze public sessions of the Council of the EU to extract how countries position themselves on different topics and where their views align.

This made it possible to create cluster maps showing potential areas for collaboration – a key starting point for identifying coalitions that could move faster on strategic initiatives such as shared European infrastructure.

👉Read the full report “Market and Power: Player or Playing Field in the Hegemony Struggle: https://lnkd.in/enPriPN4

belief: KickstartAI aims to bring together people who want to push AI forward by building on each other’s knowledge and creating positive social impact.
source_quote: we bring together people who want to push AI forward by building on each other’s knowledge and creating positive social impact
source_text:
At KickstartAI, we bring together people who want to push AI forward by building on each other’s knowledge and creating positive social impact.
As we move into 2026, we’re excited to continue our mission to accelerate AI adoption in the Netherlands and build on what we achieved in 2025:

🤝 Growing our community of AI professionals and strengthening knowledge sharing through hands-on sessions, workshops, expert blogs, and our podcast.

🚀 Opening up TechDays – our flagship AI event – to the wider Dutch ecosystem for the first time, creating space for organisations to exchange experiences and learn from one another.

⚙️ Staying focused on applying AI in real-world settings rather than in labs, we completed three projects with Ampère, Erasmus MC and Stichting Voedselbanken Nederland, and continued working with partners such as ING, KLM Royal Dutch Airlines, Nederlandse Spoorwegen, and Ahold Delhaize
on use cases ranging from detecting unattended objects at train stations to building a financial health chatbot and optimising fuel planning.

🏆 Co-organizing the National AI Challenge for the second year in a row with AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL, and working with the two winners – Leiden University Medical Center and Politie Nederland – on developing solutions for automated wound reporting and supporting the fight against environmental crime.

🏛️ Taking part in working visits with Dutch ministries to share insights on AI adoption.

Below is a glimpse of what we stand for 👇 

#AI #AINetherlands #AIAdoption #AICommunity

source_text:
🚀 Meet Julian von der Goltz, ML Engineer at KickstartAI!

With a background in aerospace engineering, computer vision, and six years working on AI-powered drone inspection systems, he enjoys exploring how machine learning can be applied in new domains.

As part of our team, he develops AI solutions with positive societal impact, from improving wound care with LUMC to building a financial-health chatbot with ING.

💡 His advice to people considering a career in AI?
“Understand how models are made and trained. It’ll help you build with or on top of AI yourself”

belief: Versnelling van AI-ontwikkeling is cruciaal voor productiviteitsverhoging.
source_quote: versnelling van AI-ontwikkeling is cruciaal voor productiviteitsverhoging
source_text:
“Data en AI is cruciaal", stelde Peter Wennink vanochtend bij de presentatie van zijn rapport 'De route naar toekomstige welvaart.'

🔸  Het is duidelijk dat urgentie van investeringen en concrete stappen op AI absoluut noodzakelijk zijn: versnelling van AI-ontwikkeling is cruciaal voor productiviteitsverhoging. 

🔸 En AI toepassing is minstens zo belangrijk, maar die blijft echter achter, zo blijkt. Nederland verliest terrein in het gebruik in bedrijven klein en groot. KickstartAI pakt die handschoen graag op. Door op grote schaal verantwoorde AI projecten te doen, bij bedrijven als KLM, Ahold maar ook in de hoogwaardige maakindustrie, in de zorg bij Erasmus MC en LUMC en met de overheid rondom de Nationale Digitalisering Strategie. 

Dit onderstreept de noodzaak van een groot transformatieprogramma waarmee we AI inzetten voor maatschappij en economie. Wij delen met Wennink dat het één voor twaalf is: voor de Nederlandse economie én voor het verantwoord versnellen van AI toepassing. 

👇 Bekijk hieronder het volledige rapport: https://lnkd.in/e8Uh3g3M

#AI #AItoepassing #AIadoptionNetherlands

belief: Collaborations between academia and societal partners are essential for NLP methods in food security to have real-world impact.
source_quote: collaborations between academia and societal partners are essential if we want these methods to have impact
source_text:
🎧 In our latest podcast, Evertjan Peer talks with Marieke Meeske (researcher at the Zero Hunger Lab at Tilburg University) about how NLP is being used in food security analysis and policymaking.

One finding from her research is hard to ignore:
🔍 Across 60+ NLP use cases in food security, only 12% ever reach deployment.
The other 88% remain in the research phase – indicating a clear implementation gap.

Marieke explains what’s behind this gap and why collaborations between academia and societal partners are essential if we want these methods to have impact. 

👉 Listen to the full conversation here: https://lnkd.in/eEixKhYb

#TheKickstartAIPodcast #AI #AIforGood

belief: KickstartAI aims to grow its impact by helping more organisations tackle concrete AI challenges, sharing what works openly with the community, and strengthening the Dutch AI ecosystem.
source_quote: Erick will focus on growing KickstartAI’s impact: helping more organisations tackle concrete AI challenges, sharing what works openly with the community, and strengthening the Dutch AI ecosystem.
source_text:
We’re excited to share that Erick Webbe will be joining KickstartAI as our new CEO starting January 1 🚀 

Erick is a familiar and trusted force within KickstartAI. As a long-time board member representing Ahold Delhaize, he has actively supported our mission to accelerate the adoption of AI in the Netherlands. He brings a deep understanding of how large organizations work, how AI moves from idea to implementation, and what it takes to connect different stakeholders around one shared goal.

In his new role as CEO, Erick will focus on growing KickstartAI’s impact: helping more organisations tackle concrete AI challenges, sharing what works openly with the community, and strengthening the Dutch AI ecosystem. 🇳🇱 

As Erick puts it, “the Netherlands must move from talking about AI to doing AI.”
“We want to show that progress doesn’t start with big words, but with tangible actions. And that a small country, if it wants to, can set a big example. I’m incredibly excited to turn our mission into reality.”

🌟 We’re looking forward to this next chapter with Erick guiding the way!

If you’d like to dive deeper into Erick’s background and vision, you can read his full interview below (link in comments) 👇 

#KickstartAI #AIAdoption #AINetherlands

belief: Helping customers improve their financial wellbeing, support those facing financial stress, and strengthen financial knowledge and resilience is an important goal for ING.
source_quote: an important step for ING in helping customers improve their financial wellbeing, support those facing financial stress, and strengthen financial knowledge and resilience
source_text:
🚀 The KickstartAI x ING Challenge is a wrap! 

Over a two-week sprint, 14 ML engineers and data scientists from KickstartAI, ING, and our partners KLM Royal Dutch Airlines, Nederlandse Spoorwegen, Ahold Delhaize, and bol joined forces to take on a bold mission: create a prototype for a financial health chatbot. 

🌟 And the outcome?

The team delivered a working prototype that operates anonymously and offers personalized educational support. It marks an important step for ING in helping customers improve their financial wellbeing, support those facing financial stress, and strengthen financial knowledge and resilience.

🇳🇱 This challenge proved once again the importance of sharing knowledge across industries and building collectively, ultimately accelerating AI adoption in the Netherlands. 

#AI #FinancialHealth #KickstartAI #ING

belief: Food security is an interconnected challenge shaped by factors like climate, biodiversity, agriculture, and nutrition.
source_quote: food security is such an interconnected challenge, shaped by factors like climate, biodiversity, agriculture, and nutrition
source_text:
🌎 How can AI support food security and the policies needed to protect it? 

🎧In our latest podcast, Evertjan Peer sits down with Marieke Meeske (PhD researcher at Zero Hunger Lab at Tilburg University) to discuss why food security is such an interconnected challenge, shaped by factors like climate, biodiversity, agriculture, and nutrition. 

Marieke shares how NLP can help make policy-relevant information easier to access and use, enabling more coordinated and evidence-based decisions.

👉 Listen to the full conversation here: https://lnkd.in/eEixKhYb

source_text:
On Monday, we wrapped up an inspiring Data Science Summit, co-organized by KickstartAI and ING. 

🌟 It was an exclusive day of sharing, learning, and connecting with our partner companies - KLM Royal Dutch Airlines, Nederlandse Spoorwegen, Ahold Delhaize, and ING - diving deep into how AI is driving real impact across industries. 

While the details stay under wraps, one thing is clear: when teams come together to openly exchange knowledge, the entire AI ecosystem grows stronger.

#DataScience #AI #DutchTech

belief: Accelerating the Dutch economy through AI requires shared learning and action.
source_quote: KickstartAI is built on the belief that accelerating the Dutch economy through AI requires shared learning and action.
source_text:
Yesterday, ING and KickstartAI - together with our partners Ahold Delhaize, KLM Royal Dutch Airlines and Nederlandse Spoorwegen - hosted the AI team of the Ministry of Economic Affairs for a working visit focused on practical AI application and adoption.

🇳🇱 KickstartAI is built on the belief that accelerating the Dutch economy through AI requires shared learning and action. During the session, our founding partners shared practical insights from the AI projects we’re carrying out together: 
🔸 What it really takes to move from idea to implementation and adoption
🔸  How to set clear goals, and how to embed AI into the core of their organisations - not as side projects, but as drivers of impact.

We discussed how organisations can strengthen their AI adoption by learning through doing, by looking at AI not only as a technology but also in terms of organisational change and broader impact, and by integrating it deeply into customer-facing and operational processes. 

We also explored how the government can help lower barriers and accelerate this transition.

⚡ Our priority at KickstartAI is clear: the urgency of AI adoption is real, and action is needed now for the Netherlands to stay competitive, economically resilient, and technologically independent.

Thanks to the Ministry for the open and constructive exchange, and to our partners for sharing their experiences so openly. The work continues!

#AI #AINetherlands #AIAdoption

Yesterday, ING and KickstartAI - together with our partners Ahold Delhaize, KLM Royal Dutch Airlines and Nederlandse Spoorwegen - hosted the AI team of the Ministry of Economic Affairs for a working visit focused on practical AI application and adoption.

🇳🇱 KickstartAI is built on the belief that accelerating the Dutch economy through AI requires shared learning and action. During the session, our founding partners shared practical insights from the AI projects we’re carrying out together: 
🔸 What it really takes to move from idea to implementation and adoption
🔸  How to set clear goals, and how to embed AI into the core of their organisations - not as side projects, but as drivers of impact.

We discussed how organisations can strengthen their AI adoption by learning through doing, by looking at AI not only as a technology but also in terms of organisational change and broader impact, and by integrating it deeply into customer-facing and operational processes. 

We also explored how the government can help lower barriers and accelerate this transition.

⚡ Our priority at KickstartAI is clear: the urgency of AI adoption is real, and action is needed now for the Netherlands to stay competitive, economically resilient, and technologically independent.

Thanks to the Ministry for the open and constructive exchange, and to our partners for sharing their experiences so openly. The work continues!

#AI #AINetherlands #AIAdoption

belief: The National AI Delta Plan is essential for the Netherlands to become a European leader in AI.
source_quote: We believe the AI Delta Plan is essential
source_text:
🇳🇱 Today, a broad coalition of AI experts presents the National AI Delta Plan – a concrete roadmap to make the Netherlands a European leader in AI through targeted investments in knowledge, talent, and infrastructure.

At KickstartAI, we strongly support this initiative and actively contribute to its vision. For years, we’ve been working hands-on with companies across sectors to move AI from experimentation to scalable impact, while cultivating one of the Netherlands’ biggest communities of AI professionals. 

📉 The Netherlands currently ranks 23rd worldwide in AI application. Without immediate and ambitious action, we risk falling further behind, with major consequences for our competitiveness, economic security, and technological sovereignty.

We believe the AI Delta Plan is essential as it calls for: 
 ✅ Accelerating AI adoption in businesses and government
 ✅ Investing in compute infrastructure and AI labs
 ✅ Attracting and training AI talent
 ✅ Supporting AI startups and scaleups
 ✅ Establishing an independent National AI Institute
 ✅ Addressing regulatory friction that limits AI adoption and startup growth 

We are committed to contributing to this mission by accelerating responsible AI adoption, fostering talent, and strengthening collaboration across the ecosystem. The time to act is now. 

👏 Shoutout to the team behind the strategy: Jelle Prins, Michiel Bakker, Renée Frissen, Stan van Baarsen, Oscar Lepoeter, Onno Eric Blom

Read more about the National AI Delta Plan below: 

https://lnkd.in/eGPw2fRz

#AI #AINetherlands #AIDeltaPlan

belief: Organisations should focus on a few priorities rather than fragmenting their AI efforts.
source_quote: Focus over fragmentation
source_text:
Very proud to see the CEOs of our founding partners featured in this insightful article by Het Financieele Dagblad!

📌 In this article, Frans Muller (Ahold Delhaize), Steven van Rijswijk (ING), Wouter Koolmees (Nederlandse Spoorwegen), and Marjan Rintel (KLM Royal Dutch Airlines) shared their lessons from deploying AI in large organizations: from reducing food waste and detecting unattended objects at train stations to fighting money laundering and predicting actual passenger numbers to better plan onboard services. 

What makes this piece special to us isn’t just the mention of KickstartAI, but the shared philosophy reflected: 

🔹 Focus over fragmentation
🔹 Start with the problem, not the technology
🔹 Don’t force AI on people, make it work for them
🔹 Combine operational reality with strategic vision
🔹 Move from isolated experiments to scalable practices

We’re proud of our partners, their honesty about what works (and what doesn’t), and how they keep pushing for AI that actually serves people, processes, and society.

👉 This is a must-read for any company navigating the shift from AI potentials to real operational impact. 

Read the full article below:
https://lnkd.in/erNQm2xt

#AI #AholdDelhaize #ING #NS #KLM #KickstartAI

belief: AI is a force for good.
source_quote: AI is a force for good
source_text:
We’re thrilled to start our project with Leiden University Medical Center to create an AI tool that improves wound care and reduces nurse’s administrative burden!

🩹 What we’re building
In collaboration with LUMC’s Cairelab and the Nursing Information Officers (NIOs), we’re developing an AI model that can generate structured wound reports directly from photos

💡 Why this matters
Nurses dedicate valuable time to wound reporting — a manual, time-consuming process, which often takes place daily. Our goal is to let AI handle the routine steps: automatically detecting the wound area, classifying tissue types, and drafting a structured TIME-style report that nurses can quickly review and validate.

The impact:
 ✅ Less administrative work
 ✅ More consistent and objective wound reports
 ✅ Better communication and handovers
 ✅ More time for direct patient care

The goal is to have the model operational in the hospital within one year. After validation, the same approach could be used in more hospitals across the Netherlands. 

This project builds on LUMC’s success as one of the winners of the National AI Challenge 2025, organized together with AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL.  As part of the prize, our team of ML engineers will help bring this idea to life — from prototype to adoption.

At KickstartAI, we believe that AI is a force for good, and we’re proud to collaborate on innovations that have real impact on care, patients, and professionals 🤝 🇳🇱 

#AIinHealthcare #Healthcare #Innovation

belief: The core purpose of the challenge is to develop a tool that helps ING customers take control of their financial health.
source_quote: The core purpose of this challenge is to develop a tool that helps ING customers take control of their financial health
source_text:
🚀 Exciting news! We're kicking off the KickstartAI x ING Challenge: Financial Health Chatbot — a collaborative initiative focused on using AI to improve financial well-being.

Over the course of two weeks, 14 participants from our team and our partner companies — ING, KLM Royal Dutch Airlines, Nederlandse Spoorwegen, Ahold Delhaize, and bol — will join forces to co-create a smart, empathetic, and accessible GenAI-powered chatbot prototype.

💡 The core purpose of this challenge is to develop a tool that helps ING customers take control of their financial health, by supporting individuals experiencing financial stress or instability, as well as those actively seeking to improve their financial knowledge.

⚙️ We’ll build a solution that understands users and offers educational guidance based on validated ING content. By operating anonymously and steering clear of specific financial advice, the chatbot has the potential to lower the barriers to seeking financial support. Ultimately, it could increase financial literacy and resilience across society. 

The challenge will serve as a launchpad for validation and scaling, paving the way toward the implementation phase.

Together with our partners, we believe in sharing knowledge, building collectively, and accelerating AI adoption across the Netherlands — all while ensuring AI is used as a force for social good.🇳🇱

#innovation #GenAI #financialhealth #collaboration

belief: Using satellite imagery to fill data gaps on clean water access and waste management supports more effective humanitarian action in vulnerable areas.
source_quote: explore how satellite imagery can help fill data gaps on clean water access and waste management — supporting more effective humanitarian action in vulnerable areas
source_text:
🌟 Don’t miss our last workshop of the year!

We’re proud to partner with 510 - The Netherlands Red Cross' data & digital team for a hands-on mini hackathon to explore how satellite imagery can help fill data gaps on clean water access and waste management — supporting more effective humanitarian action in vulnerable areas.

🗓️ What's on the agenda: 
💧 Dive into satellite images to spot potential water points like wells or tanks.
♻️ Identify visible waste issues such as plastic accumulation or open dumpsites.
 🛠️ Work in mixed teams to explore what’s feasible from space, which datasets and tools are available, and how these insights could support humanitarian or environmental action.

If you’re interested in satellite data interpretation and humanitarian data challenges, this one’s for you. Join us for an evening of inspiring collaboration and use your skills to make a real impact!

Spots are limited. RSVP below 👇 

#Data4Good #SatelliteImagery #KickstartAI

🎧 In our latest podcast, Andrii Degeler (founder of unzip.media and journalist-in-residence at Dealroom.co) joins Ioanna Lykiardopoulou to discuss how LLMs’ data needs are shifting — and what that means for news publishers.

As Andrii explains, training data is no longer a viable revenue model; the focus is moving to real-time information and how its use is controlled and priced.

We also cover: 
🧠 AI literacy & media trust
🗞️ GenAI use in newsrooms 
 ✍️ Authorship and disclosure ethics
📊 The sustainability of media business models

belief: Good data is more important than complex models.
source_quote: Good data beats complex models.
source_text:
Our AI for the Planet meetup was a special one, bringing together the KickstartAI and Xomnia's Data&Drinks communities for an evening full of energy and lively conversations!

A big thank you to Xomnia for hosting us, to everyone who joined, and to our speakers who shared their work on stage: 
🔸 Akhil Chhibber 🇮🇳 🇳🇱 applying deep learning to map deforestation in the Amazon
🔸 Inês S. Veríssimo developing AI-powered tools to monitor and protect sea turtles.
🔸 Maria Bader, Ph.D. using satellite imagery to help wildlife rangers make data-informed conservation decisions.

Our key takeaways? 
🌱 Good data beats complex models.
 🧭 Tools need to be usable, not just accurate.
 💪 There’s always a way to create real social impact.

Couldn't make it? Join our upcoming meetup on December 11: https://lnkd.in/ecMy3ueV

#AIxPlanet #AIforGood #AI

belief: KickstartAI aims to help bring promising AI ideas to life, from development to adoption.
source_quote: our team of ML engineers will help bring their AI idea to life, from development to adoption
source_text:
🚀 We’re excited to kick off our project with Politie Nederland and start working on an AI tool to support the fight against environmental crime. 

While environmental crime is the third largest criminal activity in the world, the respective laws are complex and environmental criminals know how to cover their tracks. But in doing so, they’re prone to violating other laws. 

💡 That’s why the idea is to develop a tool that can search for all relevant legislation beyond the scope of environmental law, potentially uncovering criminal offences that are easier to prove. 

Think of Al Capone, who ultimately didn’t get convicted for the many murders tied to his name, but for something entirely different: tax evasion. 

The Police was one of the winners of the National AI Challenge 2025 we organized together with AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL. As part of the prize, our team of ML engineers will help bring their AI idea to life, from development to adoption. 

At KickstartAI, we believe AI is a force for good. We look forward to continue working with the Police on a project with such far-reaching societal impact. 

⚡ Stay tuned! 

#AIforGood #AI #AISocialImpact

source_text:

That question took center stage at the Netherlands’ Tech Election Debate on October 7 — co-organized by KickstartAI, NLTech, and Techleap — where politicians, entrepreneurs, and experts came together to discuss how the Netherlands can build a future-proof digital economy and accelerate innovation.

The debate highlighted the importance of investment, talent attraction, and AI adoption as enablers of a strong digital future. 

At KickstartAI, we believe AI holds enormous potential to drive that future —  helping safeguard our welfare state, boost our economy, and strengthen our society. The urgency is clear: if we hesitate, the Netherlands risks falling behind in competitiveness and sovereignty.

💪 It’s time to act now, and do together.

belief: AI is a force for good that can safeguard the welfare state, boost the economy, and strengthen society.
source_quote: we believe AI isn’t a blocker — it’s a force for good. It can safeguard our welfare state, boost our economy, and strengthen our society.
source_text:
⚠️ What are we going to do to start AI adoption tomorrow?
That’s the key question our CEO Nils Beers posed during his speech at the Netherlands’ Tech Election Debate we co-organized last week.

At KickstartAI, we believe AI isn’t a blocker — it’s a force for good. It can safeguard our welfare state, boost our economy, and strengthen our society. The urgency is clear: if we hesitate, the Netherlands risks falling behind in competitiveness and sovereignty.

That’s why there’s no more time for endless roundtable talks, boardroom discussions, and debates in The Hague. 

It’s time to:
🔸Boost public investment by supporting frontrunners from business and from the tech ecosystem
🔸Adopt rules that support entrepreneurs instead of overburdening them with compliance and uncertainty
🔸Focus on measurable impact instead of AI pilots
🔸Create space for the doers and enable them to share their learnings

🚀 We need to act now. 
🤝 Let’s build a coalition of doing, together.

#AI #AIadoption #Coalitionofthedoing #AIadoptieNederland

We’re proud to have worked with KLM Royal Dutch Airlines on the Fuel AI Challenge ✈️ 

During an intensive two weeks, data scientists and AI engineers from KickstartAI and our partner companies, KLM, Nederlandse Spoorwegen, ING, and Ahold Delhaize, set out to explore: 

👉 How can AI help us better understand and predict the contingency fuel needed for each flight?

This challenge demonstrated not only AI's potential to bring added value, but also the power of collaboration that can further boost the Dutch ecosystem. 

Want to learn more about contingency fuel and what we discovered during the challenge? Check out the video below!

belief: Onderwijs en kennisontwikkeling vormen de basis van de toekomstige economie.
source_quote: Onderwijs en kennisontwikkeling vormen de basis van onze toekomstige economie.
source_text:
Afgelopen dinsdag organiseerden NLTech, KickstartAI en Techleap hét Nederlandse Tech verkiezingsdebat, waar kandidaat-Kamerleden en ondernemers in gesprek gingen over een toekomstbestendige digitale economie en sterk tech-ecosysteem.
 
Kandidaat-Kamerleden Wimar Bolhuis (GroenLinks-PvdA), Maes van Lanschot (CDA), Claire Martens - America (VVD), Joost Sneller (D66), Marieke Koekkoek (Volt Nederland) en Christian van der Krift, PhD (ChristenUnie) gingen het debat aan over hoe Nederland een toekomstbestendig tech ecosysteem —  van startups tot digitale kampioenen — kan bouwen en hoe we als Nederland AI-koploper kunnen worden.
 
Wat opviel tijdens de discussie over #kapitaal: vrijwel alle partijen pleitten voor meer langetermijndenken en investeringszekerheid. Niemand twijfelde aan de noodzaak om innovatie te stimuleren, maar de vraag bleef 𝘩𝘰𝘦. Er werd gepleit voor het versterken van de Europese #kapitaalunie, maar ook voor een nationale #investeringsinstelling en meer publiek-private samenwerking.
 
Onderwijs en kennisontwikkeling vormen de basis van onze toekomstige economie. Tegelijkertijd benadrukte de partijen dat fiscale regelingen als de expat-regeling cruciaal zijn voor het aantrekken van internationaal #talent, en dat aanpassing hiervan onverstandig is voor de Nederlandse concurrentiepositie binnen de EU.
 
#AI werd gezien als essentieel. VVD, GLPvdA, Volt, D66 en CU waren het eens: voor Nederland is het nu tijd voor actie om concurrerend te blijven en kansen te grijpen door snelle (en verantwoorde) AI-adoptie.

👉 Belangrijk is nu dat we deze mooie plannen en ambities terugzien in het regeerakkoord. 

#AI #AINederland

belief: Turning ideas from the AI community into real collaborations and practical results is an important goal for the organisation.
source_quote: turning these ideas into real collaborations and practical results
source_text:
TechDays 2025 was a blast 🎉
This year was extra special, as we opened the doors to the entire Dutch AI community! A great crowd of AI engineers and data scientists joined our flagship event to bring our theme — AI in Action — to life 🧡

Here are some of our highlights:
🔹 We learned from insightful talks, including:
Edwin Rijgersberg on how (not) to train open LLMs
Maarten C. Stol on the relationship between learning and reasoning in modern AI
Okke van der Wal on how AI is transforming Uber payments

🔹We dived into: 
 – The future of AI with expert from TNO and ASML
 – AI’s social impact through inspiring use cases from Otermans Institute, 510 - The Netherlands Red Cross' data & digital team, and CorrelAid Nederland
 – Critical industry applications in healthcare (Ditto) and food waste prevention (OneThird)

🔹 We rolled up our sleeves in hands-on workshops — from building guardrails for conversational AI agents to designing multi-agent systems. Huge thanks to ML6 and Cisco!

🔹 We connected — through Q&As, networking, and over plenty of food and drinks, sparking new ideas and conversations.

✨ What excites us most is what comes next: turning these ideas into real collaborations and practical results, together with this incredible community. 

Two initiatives are already taking shape — and we can’t wait to share more soon. In the meantime, here’s a video recap to relive the spirit of TechDays 2025👇

#TechDays2025 #KickstartAI #AIinNetherlands

belief: The main challenge in large organizations’ AI projects is not the technology but making the leap from a pilot to a solution that fits processes, strategy, and people.
source_quote: The challenge is rarely the technology. It’s making the leap from a pilot to a solution that truly fits processes, strategy, and people.
source_text:
📊 We recently completed a project with Erasmus MC, where we turned an AI idea into a practical tool that predicts patient inflow in their busy emergency department.

Along the way, we saw firsthand why many AI projects stall in large organizations. The challenge is rarely the technology. It’s making the leap from a pilot to a solution that truly fits processes, strategy, and people.

That’s why we distilled our experience into three core lessons – to help others bridge the gap from pilot to adoption and unlock real value with AI.

👉 Curious which lessons can really make the difference? Read the full story below: https://lnkd.in/ewT7JeeX

#AI #AIadoption #KickstartAI

belief: AI innovation is not just about technology but fundamentally about people and governance.
source_quote: AI innovation isn’t just about technology. Prototypes and pilots are valuable but the real challenge – and opportunity – lies in people and governance.
source_text:
We're proud that our CEO Nils Beers represented KickstartAI at the Tweede Kamer during the roundtable discussion on the National Digitalization Strategy.

In his remarks, he emphasized an important truth: AI innovation isn’t just about technology. Prototypes and pilots are valuable but the real challenge – and opportunity – lies in people and governance.

🔸 Technology is rarely the barrier; adoption depends on how we bring people along.
 🔸 Pilots are valuable to test products, but true impact comes from scaling and embedding solutions.
 🔸 That requires clear strategies, strong governance, and above all, engaging stakeholders from the very beginning

Digital transformation in the Netherlands will succeed not only because of our technological capabilities, but because of our ability to collaborate, adapt, and change together. Now is the time to act. 

#Digitalisering #TweedeKamer #KickstartAI #AI

belief: The Netherlands should remain a leader in technology rather than fall behind.

source_text:
🚀 Groei of Stilstand — hét Nederlandse Tech Debat
Op 7 oktober draait alles bij The Hague Tech om de vraag: hoe blijft Nederland koploper in tech?

NLtech, Techleap en KickstartAI organiseren hét verkiezingsdebat over de toekomst van het Nederlandse tech ecosysteem.

🇳🇱 Nederland hoort bij de digitale top van Europa. Maar de vraag is: houden we die voorsprong vast, of raken we achterop? En hoe gaan we van praten naar doen? 

Kandidaat-Kamerleden waaronder Claire Martens - America (VVD), Wimar Bolhuis (GroenLinks-PvdA) en Joost Sneller (D66) gaan de strijd aan over:
 
 👉 Hoe bouwen we een toekomstbestendig tech ecosysteem?
 👉 Wat hebben ondernemers écht nodig (talent, kapitaal, regels)?
 👉 Hoe maken we Nederland koploper in AI?

Reken op scherpe stellingen, pittige debatten en volop ruimte voor ondernemers en publiek om mee te doen.

 🗓️ 7 oktober 2025 | 15.00–17.30 (incl. netwerkborrel)
 📍 The Hague Tech, Den Haag
 
Kies voor groei, niet voor stilstand. Meld je nu aan en claim je plek hier aan:
🔗 https://lnkd.in/dNvzw6mR
Dit wil je niet missen!

belief: Scaling the detection of unattended objects such as suitcases is critical for station safety.
source_quote: scaling the detection of unattended objects such as suitcases is critical for station safety
source_text:
Last week, Sophia Claire Zitman took the stage at Data Expo 2025 to share how KickstartAI and Nederlandse Spoorwegen are working together to improve safety at Dutch train stations using computer vision.

The session took the audience behind the scenes of a real-world case study:
🔸 Why scaling the detection of unattended objects such as suitcases is critical for station safety
🔸 How the idea turned into an operational AI solution
🔸 Technical hurdles of running vision models in a low-latency environment
🔸 Organizational & ethical challenges of using privacy-sensitive video data

The big takeaway? Responsibly deploying AI in high-stake settings isn’t about the technology. It’s about trust and collaboration, laying the foundation for broader AI adoption in the public domain.

#DataExpo2025 #AI #ComputerVision #PublicSafety

source_quote: isn’t about the technology. It’s about trust and collaboration
source_text:
Last week, Sophia Claire Zitman took the stage at Data Expo 2025 to share how KickstartAI and Nederlandse Spoorwegen are working together to improve safety at Dutch train stations using computer vision.

The session took the audience behind the scenes of a real-world case study:
🔸 Why scaling the detection of unattended objects such as suitcases is critical for station safety
🔸 How the idea turned into an operational AI solution
🔸 Technical hurdles of running vision models in a low-latency environment
🔸 Organizational & ethical challenges of using privacy-sensitive video data

The big takeaway? Responsibly deploying AI in high-stake settings isn’t about the technology. It’s about trust and collaboration, laying the foundation for broader AI adoption in the public domain.

#DataExpo2025 #AI #ComputerVision #PublicSafety

belief: Focusing on people, not just algorithms, is essential for AI to succeed.
source_quote: focusing on the people too (beyond just the algorithms) is essential for AI to succeed
source_text:
Our CEO Nils Beers was recently featured in an article by Het Financieele Dagblad, discussing one of the biggest challenges in AI adoption: not the technology itself, but the human factor.

The article explores how trust, organizational structure, and implementation mistakes can become barriers — and why focusing on the people too (beyond just the algorithms) is essential for AI to succeed.

At KickstartAI, we’re proud to be part of this important conversation, and we’re dedicated to bridging the gap between AI talk and action.

👇 Read the full article below: https://lnkd.in/exMkQdS7

belief: AI should be used to ease the administrative burden on nurses and improve the quality of care.
source_quote: use AI to generate structured wound reports from photos, aiming to ease the administrative burden on nurses and improve the quality of care
source_text:
✨ We have our winners! ✨

We’re thrilled to announce that Leiden University Medical Center (via its Cairelab) and Politie Nederland have won the National AI Challenge 2025, a unique collaboration between KickstartAI and AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL. 

🏆 Leiden University Medical Center impressed the jury with their idea to use AI to generate structured wound reports from photos, aiming to ease the administrative burden on nurses and improve the quality of care.

"We're super happy we won this challenge! We hope that  this solution becomes a standard support tool in the wound care process — available to every nurse, in every care setting," said Albert Simonse, Nursing Information Officer at LUMC. 

🏆 Politie Nederland stood out with their vision to use AI to cut through complex environmental legislation and help investigators leverage all available laws so that they can hold polluters accountable.

"I'm very happy that we won! This AI tool will greatly help strengthen our capabilities, enabling us to take down the biggest environmental criminals faster and more decisively," said Amir Niknam, Senior Advisor at the Police. 

🔜 Over the coming months, our team will work closely with both LUMC and the Police to bring their ideas to life — from concept to fully working AI solutions.

A huge congratulations to our winners, and a heartfelt thanks to our other outstanding finalists (Gemeente Den Haag, Instituut Verantwoord Medicijngebruik, Leiden University Medical Center via its Emergency Department) for their dedication and vision! 

👏 A big shoutout to our expert jury as well (Marelle van Beerschoten, Thomas Dohmen, Thomas Faber, Herman Kienhuis, Janneke Niessen) for their contribution! 

👉 For more info, check out our press release: https://lnkd.in/eKzhzawG

#NationalAIChallenge #KickstartAI #AIC4NL #AIForGood

belief: AI can be used to address compelling real-world problems and make a positive difference.
source_quote: compelling real-world problems they believe AI can help solve
source_text:
The countdown is officially on! ⏳ We are just days away from selecting the winner of the National AI Challenge, a unique collaboration between KickstartAI and AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL.

Our five finalists have presented compelling real-world problems they believe AI can help solve. Their ambition to make a difference is truly inspiring:

- Gemeente Den Haag: Aims to use AI to analyze inspection photos and write reports in order to reduce administrative tasks for housing inspectors, freeing up time for critical fieldwork.
- Instituut Verantwoord Medicijngebruik: Envisions an intelligent engine to more efficiently screen medication incident reports, aiming to reduce manual work and provide faster, more widespread insights for healthcare organisations and professionals.
- Leiden University Medical Center (via its Cairelab): Seeks an AI-supported solution to generate structured wound reports from photos, aiming to ease the burden on nurses and improve the quality of care.
- Politie Nederland: Proposes an AI tool to cut through complex environmental legislation and help investigators leverage all available laws so that they can hold polluters accountable.
- Leiden University Medical Center (via its ED department): Envisions an AI-powered TriageTool that will allow patients to perform their own initial triage by filling in information on a (medical) device. This would enable a more objective and faster assessment, freeing up triage nurses to assist with other critical tasks.

The winner will receive a custom-built AI solution from A to Z, developed by our team. Our expert jury – Marelle van Beerschoten, Herman Kienhuis, Thomas Dohmen, Thomas Faber and Janneke Niessen — will make the final decision.

Stay tuned for the winner announcement next week, to find out which organization will have its AI solution brought to life! 

#NationalAIChallenge #KickstartAI #AIforGood #AI

belief: Scaling advanced AI and generative AI requires a fundamental, cloud-first architectural shift built on principles of governance and security.
source_quote: scaling advanced AI and GenAI requires a fundamental, cloud-first architectural shift built on principles of governance and security
source_text:
A massive thank you to everyone who came out to our AI in Finance meetup last night! It was fantastic to talk tech, share a cocktail, and catch up. We're also very grateful to Eraneos Netherlands for partnering with us to make the event happen.

For those who couldn't make it, or for anyone who wants a recap, here are three key takeaways from the evening:

1️⃣ Infrastructure is the foundation: Federico Calore (ING) shared the blueprint for ING's next-gen data platform, VISTA, proving that scaling advanced AI and GenAI requires a fundamental, cloud-first architectural shift built on principles of governance and security.
2️⃣ AI for compliance is here: Gergely Bognár (Eraneos) demonstrated a GenAI framework that automates legal compliance checks for the Dutch pension market, ensuring both accuracy and completeness against legislation.
3️⃣ Transparency is non-negotiable: Tim Kleinloog (Deeploy) highlighted that in the fight against financial crime, AI models must be transparent, fair, and explainable to be truly effective and trustworthy in a regulated world.

💡 The big theme of the night was clear: we're moving from AI hype to practical, responsible implementation. 

It was a great way to close out the summer season, and now we're all fired up and ready to roll up our sleeves for the busy months ahead. See you all at the next one! 

Next up: 
AI x Education: Rewiring learning, Thursday, September 18 in Amsterdam

belief: AI should be used to help doctors and nurses better anticipate and manage patient inflow.
source_quote: How can AI help doctors and nurses better anticipate and manage patient inflow?
source_text:
Nine months ago, we kicked off an exciting journey with the Emergency Department (ED) of Erasmus MC, the winner of the National AI Challenge 2024. Together, we set out to tackle a critical issue: unpredictable patient inflow and limited treatment capacity.

Our question: How can AI help doctors and nurses better anticipate and manage patient inflow?

Over the course of the project:
🩺 Shadowed 5 shifts in the ED (in full nurse uniforms!) and interviewed dozens of doctors and nurses.
 🤝 Collaborated across 8 departments to combine medical, organizational, and technical expertise.
 📚 Hosted learning sessions to share insights and build AI knowledge inside the hospital.

The result? An AI-powered dashboard that:
✅ Predicts expected patient inflow in real time
✅ Provides long-term trends for smarter scheduling
✅ Helps staff shift from reacting to crises to managing resources proactively

For us, this project was about much more than building technology. It was about collaboration, empathy, and learning how AI can truly support healthcare professionals where it matters most: in patient care.

A big thank you to everyone at Erasmus MC and all the teams involved in making this happen. 👏 

#AI #NationalAIChallenge #KickstartAI

belief: The industry's label of LLMs as 'reasoning models' is misleading.
source_quote: the industry's label is misleading
source_text:
🤔 Should we be calling LLMs "reasoning" models?

Niya Stoimenova, who holds a PhD in AI reasoning, says the answer is no. In our new Community Spotlight, she explains why the industry's label is misleading. She breaks down deduction, induction, and abduction — and makes a compelling case for what's missing in today’s AI.

And the gap she points to? It may be the very thing that separates smart AI from real AGI. 

👉 This is a must read for anyone researching, building, or applying AI. 
📖 Read the full story below: 

https://lnkd.in/exucyzfz  

#AI #AGI #LLMs
