🚀 PyData Amsterdam 2025 is a wrap! Here are some of our highlights:

🎤  A keynote by our Tech Lead Evertjan Peer on detecting unattended objects at train stations. As part of our project with NS Stations (onderdeel Nederlandse Spoorwegen), EJ showed how the team built a full detection pipeline: from data anonymization and preprocessing to a labeling platform, YOLO-based object detection, and real-time tracking.

🙌 Meeting new people at our community booth and connecting with fellow coders, data scientists, and AI engineers.

 🧩 Hearing about fresh techniques for RAG projects and cool Python notebooks, and exploring the many use cases for building on pre-trained foundation models.

source_text:
We're proud that our CEO Nils Beers represented KickstartAI at the Tweede Kamer during the roundtable discussion on the National Digitalization Strategy.

In his remarks, he emphasized an important truth: AI innovation isn’t just about technology. Prototypes and pilots are valuable but the real challenge – and opportunity – lies in people and governance.

🔸 Technology is rarely the barrier; adoption depends on how we bring people along.
 🔸 Pilots are valuable to test products, but true impact comes from scaling and embedding solutions.
 🔸 That requires clear strategies, strong governance, and above all, engaging stakeholders from the very beginning

Digital transformation in the Netherlands will succeed not only because of our technological capabilities, but because of our ability to collaborate, adapt, and change together. Now is the time to act. 

#Digitalisering #TweedeKamer #KickstartAI #AI

source_text:
At TechDays 2025, we’re bringing two workshops you can’t miss if you’re building (or want to build) with conversational AI and multi-agent systems. 

⚙️ Workshop 1: 
Learn how to secure conversational AI by designing, implementing, and testing guardrails that block malicious content from chatbot applications. Approaches will include task-specific ML models, LLM-based solutions, and rule-based methods. 

⚙️ Workshop 2: 
Using AGNTCY’s CoffeeAgntcy reference implementation, you’ll build and run your own multi-agent system simulating a coffee supply chain. You’ll see a supervisor agent communicate with multiple agents via the SLIM protocol and even extend the setup by adding your own servers or components. 

The workshops will be led by ML6 | Your partner in AI (powered by Cisco) — who will share real lessons from the field and guide you through the hands-on labs. Cas Coopman, Cristóbal Sendín, Thomas Vrancken, Tom Istaz, Boaz Kwakkel

👉 Spots are limited! Register for TechDays 2025 and the workshops here: https://lnkd.in/e8Y9PJDA

👀PS: Attendance is free for AI professionals (valued at €1,000)

#TechDays2025 #AIinAction #AIworkshops

📣 TechDays 2025 speaker drop! Don’t miss the keynote by Maarten C. Stol,  Chief Scientific Officer at Braincreators. 

In his keynote, Stol will challenge us to rethink the choices that shaped today's AI. From the drawbacks of benchmark-driven AI research to the GPU hardware lottery of deep learning and the "bitter lesson" debate, he'll explore how things might have unfolded differently – and what that means for the future. 

He'll also tackle big questions: 
🔸 How do we get the best ROI from current AI research? 
🔸 How can entrepreneurs benefit from the AI boom beyond the hype? 
🔸 What might building a successful AI product look like in 2035?

🔥 Our meetup on AI & Education is a wrap! A big thank you to everyone who joined us yesterday, and to Codam College for hosting the event! It was an insightful evening full of engaging talks and great conversations over drinks.

Here’s a quick recap of what we covered: 

🔹 Johannes Schleiss (Program Manager AI & Data in Education, SURF)
introduced SURF’s research on building a responsible AI ecosystem for Dutch higher education.
🔹 Nicholas Hogan (AI Team Lead, Futurewhiz) & Sumesh Dubey (AI Engineer, Futurewhiz) shared their journey in building mastery-modelled recommendations for personalized learning. They explained their pivot from traditional systems that hit a performance ceiling to building a conversational AI tutor. 
🔹 Ali Pala (co-founder, MyTaco AI) took us behind the scenes of the team's personalized voice tutors: from real-time, cross-browser voice interactions to semantic voice detection, multi-dimensional language assessments, and prompt engineering across six languages.

📣 Next up: TechDays 2025, our flagship AI conference. 
📅 October 02

source_text:
📣 The speaker drop for TechDays 2025 begins 📣 

We're starting off strong with Edwin Rijgersberg, ML Engineer and the creator of GEITje-7B: the first open Dutch LLM. 

GEITje-7B was hailed as important step for Dutch AI and found its way into scientific papers, paving the road for a wave of Dutch open models. But about a year after its creation, GEITje-7B also became the first model removed under threat of litigation over its training data. 

Now, as the AI Act reshapes the field, Rijgersberg will take the stage to tell the story behind GEITje-7B's rise and fall — and share how (not) to train an LLM in 2025. 

👀 More speaker drops to follow! 
👉  Save your spot and register for TechDays 2025: https://lnkd.in/e8Y9PJDA
😉 PS: Attendance is free for AI professionals (valued at €1,000)

#TechDays2025 #AI #AIinTheNetherlands

source_text:
🚀 Groei of Stilstand — hét Nederlandse Tech Debat
Op 7 oktober draait alles bij The Hague Tech om de vraag: hoe blijft Nederland koploper in tech?

NLtech, Techleap en KickstartAI organiseren hét verkiezingsdebat over de toekomst van het Nederlandse tech ecosysteem.

🇳🇱 Nederland hoort bij de digitale top van Europa. Maar de vraag is: houden we die voorsprong vast, of raken we achterop? En hoe gaan we van praten naar doen? 

Kandidaat-Kamerleden waaronder Claire Martens - America (VVD), Wimar Bolhuis (GroenLinks-PvdA) en Joost Sneller (D66) gaan de strijd aan over:
 
 👉 Hoe bouwen we een toekomstbestendig tech ecosysteem?
 👉 Wat hebben ondernemers écht nodig (talent, kapitaal, regels)?
 👉 Hoe maken we Nederland koploper in AI?

Reken op scherpe stellingen, pittige debatten en volop ruimte voor ondernemers en publiek om mee te doen.

 🗓️ 7 oktober 2025 | 15.00–17.30 (incl. netwerkborrel)
 📍 The Hague Tech, Den Haag
 
Kies voor groei, niet voor stilstand. Meld je nu aan en claim je plek hier aan:
🔗 https://lnkd.in/dNvzw6mR
Dit wil je niet missen!

belief: Public-sector AI initiatives will only scale successfully if stakeholders perceive the deployment as trustworthy and collaborative rather than purely technical.
source_quote: isn’t about the technology. It’s about trust and collaboration, laying the foundation for broader AI adoption in the public domain.
inference_reasoning: By contrasting technology with “trust and collaboration” as the real foundation for broader AI adoption, the text presupposes that stakeholder perceptions of trust and collaboration are the strategic lever for scaling public-sector AI, not technical sophistication alone.
source_text:
Last week, Sophia Claire Zitman took the stage at Data Expo 2025 to share how KickstartAI and Nederlandse Spoorwegen are working together to improve safety at Dutch train stations using computer vision.

The session took the audience behind the scenes of a real-world case study:
🔸 Why scaling the detection of unattended objects such as suitcases is critical for station safety
🔸 How the idea turned into an operational AI solution
🔸 Technical hurdles of running vision models in a low-latency environment
🔸 Organizational & ethical challenges of using privacy-sensitive video data

The big takeaway? Responsibly deploying AI in high-stake settings isn’t about the technology. It’s about trust and collaboration, laying the foundation for broader AI adoption in the public domain.

#DataExpo2025 #AI #ComputerVision #PublicSafety

belief: Using computer vision on privacy-sensitive video data in public spaces is inherently risky and therefore requires special organizational and ethical safeguards.

inference_reasoning: Describing the use of privacy-sensitive video data as an “ethical challenge” presupposes that such applications are intrinsically fraught with risk and must be handled with heightened ethical and organizational care, beyond ordinary data use.

Our CEO Nils Beers was recently featured in an article by Het Financieele Dagblad, discussing one of the biggest challenges in AI adoption: not the technology itself, but the human factor.

The article explores how trust, organizational structure, and implementation mistakes can become barriers — and why focusing on the people too (beyond just the algorithms) is essential for AI to succeed.

At KickstartAI, we’re proud to be part of this important conversation, and we’re dedicated to bridging the gap between AI talk and action.

👇 Read the full article below: https://lnkd.in/exMkQdS7

Sophia Claire Zitman, who leads our AI projects, did a podcast with AIToday Live | AI-praktijkverhalen die je vooruit helpen, where she shared key lessons from our work with both corporate and non-profit organizations.

source_text:
What does it mean to “virtually unroll” a 2,000-year-old scroll?

Imagine a CT scan of a brain: layer by layer, more structures emerge. Now apply that idea to carbonised papyri, add segmentation software, and you’ve got the core of the Vesuvius Challenge.

The result? A way to unlock texts unseen for millenia.

🎧 We dive into it in our latest podcast with Cascha van Wanrooij, Evertjan Peer and Ioanna Lykiardopoulou

👉 Listen to the full conversation: https://lnkd.in/eWcaJkJx

#TheKickstartAIpodcast #VesuviusChallenge #AI

belief: Artificial intelligence is an appropriate and desirable tool for addressing complex societal and public-sector challenges such as healthcare quality and environmental crime.
source_quote: a unique collaboration between KickstartAI and AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL
inference_reasoning: By highlighting AI-focused organisations collaborating with hospitals and police on wound care and environmental crime, the text presupposes that AI is a fitting and positive means to tackle these societal issues, not something whose applicability is in question.
source_text:
✨ We have our winners! ✨

We’re thrilled to announce that Leiden University Medical Center (via its Cairelab) and Politie Nederland have won the National AI Challenge 2025, a unique collaboration between KickstartAI and AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL. 

🏆 Leiden University Medical Center impressed the jury with their idea to use AI to generate structured wound reports from photos, aiming to ease the administrative burden on nurses and improve the quality of care.

"We're super happy we won this challenge! We hope that  this solution becomes a standard support tool in the wound care process — available to every nurse, in every care setting," said Albert Simonse, Nursing Information Officer at LUMC. 

🏆 Politie Nederland stood out with their vision to use AI to cut through complex environmental legislation and help investigators leverage all available laws so that they can hold polluters accountable.

"I'm very happy that we won! This AI tool will greatly help strengthen our capabilities, enabling us to take down the biggest environmental criminals faster and more decisively," said Amir Niknam, Senior Advisor at the Police. 

🔜 Over the coming months, our team will work closely with both LUMC and the Police to bring their ideas to life — from concept to fully working AI solutions.

A huge congratulations to our winners, and a heartfelt thanks to our other outstanding finalists (Gemeente Den Haag, Instituut Verantwoord Medicijngebruik, Leiden University Medical Center via its Emergency Department) for their dedication and vision! 

👏 A big shoutout to our expert jury as well (Marelle van Beerschoten, Thomas Dohmen, Thomas Faber, Herman Kienhuis, Janneke Niessen) for their contribution! 

👉 For more info, check out our press release: https://lnkd.in/eKzhzawG

#NationalAIChallenge #KickstartAI #AIC4NL #AIForGood

source_text:
💡 How can AI help detect ink on 2,000-year-old carbonized texts?

This question is at the core of the Vesuvius Challenge — a global competition to virtually unroll and read the Herculaneum scrolls buried by Mt. Vesuvius in 79 AD.

🎧 In our latest podcast, Evertjan Peer, Cascha van Wanrooij, and Ioanna Lykiardopoulou explain how a team of university students trained a model to detect ink by producing a segmentation map from the CT scans of the burned papyri.

But how did they make sure that the model wasn’t hallucinating? And why did they choose TimeSformer for training?

👉 Listen to the full discussion here: https://lnkd.in/eWcaJkJx

#TheKickstartAIpodcast #AI #VesuviusChallenge

belief: The organisation assumes that AI models are prone to hallucination and that managing this risk is a central concern in serious AI applications.

source_quote: reduce administrative tasks for housing inspectors, freeing up time for critical fieldwork
inference_reasoning: Multiple examples (inspectors, medication screening, wound reporting, triage) frame manual work as a problem and AI as the remedy, implying an assumed background condition of excessive, suboptimal manual workload that ought to be automated.
source_text:
The countdown is officially on! ⏳ We are just days away from selecting the winner of the National AI Challenge, a unique collaboration between KickstartAI and AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL.

Our five finalists have presented compelling real-world problems they believe AI can help solve. Their ambition to make a difference is truly inspiring:

- Gemeente Den Haag: Aims to use AI to analyze inspection photos and write reports in order to reduce administrative tasks for housing inspectors, freeing up time for critical fieldwork.
- Instituut Verantwoord Medicijngebruik: Envisions an intelligent engine to more efficiently screen medication incident reports, aiming to reduce manual work and provide faster, more widespread insights for healthcare organisations and professionals.
- Leiden University Medical Center (via its Cairelab): Seeks an AI-supported solution to generate structured wound reports from photos, aiming to ease the burden on nurses and improve the quality of care.
- Politie Nederland: Proposes an AI tool to cut through complex environmental legislation and help investigators leverage all available laws so that they can hold polluters accountable.
- Leiden University Medical Center (via its ED department): Envisions an AI-powered TriageTool that will allow patients to perform their own initial triage by filling in information on a (medical) device. This would enable a more objective and faster assessment, freeing up triage nurses to assist with other critical tasks.

The winner will receive a custom-built AI solution from A to Z, developed by our team. Our expert jury – Marelle van Beerschoten, Herman Kienhuis, Thomas Dohmen, Thomas Faber and Janneke Niessen — will make the final decision.

Stay tuned for the winner announcement next week, to find out which organization will have its AI solution brought to life! 

#NationalAIChallenge #KickstartAI #AIforGood #AI

source_text:
The countdown is officially on! ⏳ We are just days away from selecting the winner of the National AI Challenge, a unique collaboration between KickstartAI and AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL.

Our five finalists have presented compelling real-world problems they believe AI can help solve. Their ambition to make a difference is truly inspiring:

- Gemeente Den Haag: Aims to use AI to analyze inspection photos and write reports in order to reduce administrative tasks for housing inspectors, freeing up time for critical fieldwork.
- Instituut Verantwoord Medicijngebruik: Envisions an intelligent engine to more efficiently screen medication incident reports, aiming to reduce manual work and provide faster, more widespread insights for healthcare organisations and professionals.
- Leiden University Medical Center (via its Cairelab): Seeks an AI-supported solution to generate structured wound reports from photos, aiming to ease the burden on nurses and improve the quality of care.
- Politie Nederland: Proposes an AI tool to cut through complex environmental legislation and help investigators leverage all available laws so that they can hold polluters accountable.
- Leiden University Medical Center (via its ED department): Envisions an AI-powered TriageTool that will allow patients to perform their own initial triage by filling in information on a (medical) device. This would enable a more objective and faster assessment, freeing up triage nurses to assist with other critical tasks.

The winner will receive a custom-built AI solution from A to Z, developed by our team. Our expert jury – Marelle van Beerschoten, Herman Kienhuis, Thomas Dohmen, Thomas Faber and Janneke Niessen — will make the final decision.

Stay tuned for the winner announcement next week, to find out which organization will have its AI solution brought to life! 

#NationalAIChallenge #KickstartAI #AIforGood #AI

source_text:
A massive thank you to everyone who came out to our AI in Finance meetup last night! It was fantastic to talk tech, share a cocktail, and catch up. We're also very grateful to Eraneos Netherlands for partnering with us to make the event happen.

For those who couldn't make it, or for anyone who wants a recap, here are three key takeaways from the evening:

1️⃣ Infrastructure is the foundation: Federico Calore (ING) shared the blueprint for ING's next-gen data platform, VISTA, proving that scaling advanced AI and GenAI requires a fundamental, cloud-first architectural shift built on principles of governance and security.
2️⃣ AI for compliance is here: Gergely Bognár (Eraneos) demonstrated a GenAI framework that automates legal compliance checks for the Dutch pension market, ensuring both accuracy and completeness against legislation.
3️⃣ Transparency is non-negotiable: Tim Kleinloog (Deeploy) highlighted that in the fight against financial crime, AI models must be transparent, fair, and explainable to be truly effective and trustworthy in a regulated world.

💡 The big theme of the night was clear: we're moving from AI hype to practical, responsible implementation. 

It was a great way to close out the summer season, and now we're all fired up and ready to roll up our sleeves for the busy months ahead. See you all at the next one! 

Next up: 
AI x Education: Rewiring learning, Thursday, September 18 in Amsterdam

source_text:
🔥 What do AI, a volcano, and ancient texts have in common?

In our latest podcast, Evertjan Peer, Cascha van Wanrooij, and Ioanna Lykiardopoulou break down the Vesuvius Challenge — an ongoing competition that combines ML, computer vision, and X-ray tomography to read 2,000-year-old scrolls turned into charcoal by Vesuvius’ eruption in 79AD.

They discuss how the winning team of the Grand Prize 2023 improved virtual unrolling and ink detection, borrowing techniques from video models to revive lost words of the philosopher Philodemus of Gadara. 

🎧 History meets AI! Tune in to the full discussion: https://lnkd.in/eWcaJkJx

#TheKickstartAIpodcast #VesuviusChallenge #AI

belief: Effective AI solutions in healthcare require deep, on-the-ground understanding of clinical workflows and staff experiences, not just technical development.
source_quote: Shadowed 5 shifts in the ED (in full nurse uniforms!) and interviewed dozens of doctors and nurses.
inference_reasoning: By emphasizing shadowing shifts and interviewing staff as a core part of the project, the text presupposes that close immersion in clinical practice is strategically necessary for building meaningful AI tools.
source_text:
Nine months ago, we kicked off an exciting journey with the Emergency Department (ED) of Erasmus MC, the winner of the National AI Challenge 2024. Together, we set out to tackle a critical issue: unpredictable patient inflow and limited treatment capacity.

Our question: How can AI help doctors and nurses better anticipate and manage patient inflow?

Over the course of the project:
🩺 Shadowed 5 shifts in the ED (in full nurse uniforms!) and interviewed dozens of doctors and nurses.
 🤝 Collaborated across 8 departments to combine medical, organizational, and technical expertise.
 📚 Hosted learning sessions to share insights and build AI knowledge inside the hospital.

The result? An AI-powered dashboard that:
✅ Predicts expected patient inflow in real time
✅ Provides long-term trends for smarter scheduling
✅ Helps staff shift from reacting to crises to managing resources proactively

For us, this project was about much more than building technology. It was about collaboration, empathy, and learning how AI can truly support healthcare professionals where it matters most: in patient care.

A big thank you to everyone at Erasmus MC and all the teams involved in making this happen. 👏 

#AI #NationalAIChallenge #KickstartAI

source_text:
🔥 Our latest podcast matched summer temperatures as we moved from ashes to algorithms and how AI is resurrecting ancient 2,000-year-old texts.

How do you virtually unroll a scroll that would crumble to dust if opened? And how do you train a model to spot invisible, carbon-based ink?

🎙️ In our new episode, Evertjan Peer, Cascha van Wanrooij, and Ioanna Lykiardopoulou unpack the Vesuvius Challenge — an ongoing competition where machine learning, computer vision, and X-ray tomography come together to read scrolls buried in 79 AD after Mount Vesuvius’ eruption.

They break down how the winning team of the Grand Prize 2023 improved  virtual unrolling and ink detection, borrowing techniques from video models to revive the words of the philosopher Philodemus of Gadara, once thought lost.

📖 It’s a story of history meeting AI, with breakthroughs that could transform how we recover fragile texts across the world!

🎧 Listen to full podcast below:
https://lnkd.in/eWcaJkJx

#TheKickstartAIpodcast #AI #VesuviusChallenge

🤔 Should we be calling LLMs "reasoning" models?

Niya Stoimenova, who holds a PhD in AI reasoning, says the answer is no. In our new Community Spotlight, she explains why the industry's label is misleading. She breaks down deduction, induction, and abduction — and makes a compelling case for what's missing in today’s AI.

And the gap she points to? It may be the very thing that separates smart AI from real AGI. 

👉 This is a must read for anyone researching, building, or applying AI. 
📖 Read the full story below: 

https://lnkd.in/exucyzfz  

#AI #AGI #LLMs

belief: Regulation of AI should aim to enhance and support AI systems rather than hinder their development or performance.
source_quote: So are we writing rules that strengthen these systems — or slow them down?

source_text:
⚠️ What if we went on an AI strike for just 48 hours?
No search. No logistics. No fraud detection. No diagnostics.

That’s the idea that Nils Beers breaks down in our latest podcast to demonstrate that AI isn’t hypothetical. It’s foundational.

And that’s exactly why the EU’s AI Act matters, and why it has to get this moment right. We’re not regulating an emerging technology anymore. We’re setting the rules for systems our economy and society already depend on.

So are we writing rules that strengthen these systems — or slow them down?

🎧 Listen to the full conversation (https://lnkd.in/eMXWnPE6) and share your thoughts in the comments!

#AIAct #AI #TheKickstartAIpodcast

🚨 Our new technical blog is live!
 We explore how we used network analysis and LLMs to map the Netherlands’ threat landscape.

As part of our contribution to DenkWerk’s new report “Resilience by Design,” our ML engineer Cascha van Wanrooij built a node analysis tool to uncover how national threats interconnect, amplify each other, and where intervention can have the greatest impact.

In this post, we dive into:
🔹 Using LLMs to mine causal relationships from thousands of government and research documents
🔹 Applying centrality metrics to find the most influential risks
🔹 Creating an interactive graph for exploring systemic threats
🔹 Tacking the statistical challenge of rare events 
🔹 Why static websites with rich interactivity are the sweet spot for LLM-assisted development  

💡 Tip: Don’t underestimate smaller LLMs like Gemini 2.5 Flash — they’re fast, efficient, and very capable for this kind of semantic extraction work.

As Sophia Claire Zitman pointed out at TNW Conference, AI is too big to comprehend and too different in nature from what we’re used to. And that is why so many organizations struggle to turn potential into impact.

source_text:
🧠 The smartest thing humans do? Ignore most of the data.

In our latest podcast, Evertjan Peer, Cascha van Wanrooij, and Paul Kuyer explore what sets human intelligence apart from AI — and why that difference matters in practice.

AI learns by consuming massive amounts of data. But humans evolved to ignore most information, and act on the small fraction that matters the most.

The contrast goes beyond how we process information. It also shapes what we prioritize, remember, and understand, which influences how we behave. The AI systems we’re currently using have different foundations:
🔎  Current AI architectures don't have an integrated, persistent (and autobiographical) long-term memory
⚙️ Current models operate without intent or self-awareness
📉 But they’re still being used to generate decisions and influence at scale

So what kind of guardrails do we need when deploying systems that lack the constraints and instincts that shaped human cognition?

#TheKickstartAIPodcast #AI #ArtificialIntelligence

belief: Whether or not the brain is meaningfully comparable to a computer is a foundational issue for how people should think about AGI.
source_quote: a core analogy that sits at the heart of the AGI debate

source_text:
🧠 Is our brain a biological computer?

In our latest podcast, Evertjan Peer and Cascha van Wanrooij sit down with Paul Kuyer to unpack a core analogy that sits at the heart of the AGI debate: If the human brain is a biological computer, why can’t a digital computer eventually do all the same things and reach superhuman levels?

While the premise is plausible, the logic behind it also risks being self-reinforcing — assuming the thing it needs to prove. 

Yes, neural networks are powerful. They outperform us in specific tasks. But, at the moment at least, the technology lacks the essential traits that define human cognition: consciousness, wants, and desires.

💬 Are we anthropomorphizing models just because they speak our language? Or do the similarities between brains and machines run deeper than we’re comfortable admitting? And if they do, what kind of caution does this call for? 

👉 Listen to the full discussion here:https://lnkd.in/eFGHnCyE

#TheKickstartAIpodcast #AGI #AI

source_text:
AI holds a €85bn potential for the Dutch economy, but as Sophia Claire Zitman pointed out at TNW Conference, adoption is lagging.

It’s not about the technology, talent, or compute. It’s that AI is still stuck in the "potential" phase — too abstract, too big, too misaligned with how organizations actually work.

To unlock its impact, we have to stop treating AI like another IT upgrade. We have to rethink how we work and operate. We need to move from traditional metrics like OKRs and KPIs, to focusing on the people within organizations who are ready to reimagine what’s possible — and act on it.

In our projects, this approach has led to real results:
✈️  KLM Royal Dutch Airlines cut food waste by 65%
🛍️ bol improved parcel delivery efficiency by 6%
🚉 Nederlandse Spoorwegen train stations increasing safety 

👉 The potential is clear. The time to move from exploration to execution is now.

#KicstartAI #AIinNetherlands #AIadoption

At yesterday's KickstartAI x BigData Republic meetup, we brought together the community to explore how EU's AI Act is moving from legal text to real implications for AI teams on the ground.

A big thank you to our speakers:
🔸 Kaan K. and Cas Mevissen (Policy Officers on AI, Dutch Ministries of Justice & Economic Affairs) for unpacking the Act’s core elements and sharing how the Dutch government plans to implement it.
 🔸 Jurriaan Parie (Algorithm Audit) for showing us what public ML audits look like in practice.
 🔸 Rens van Dongen (Nederlandse Spoorwegen) for sharing his insights on how NS is implementing an internal AI Management System to embed security and ethics. 

See you at our next meetup at the end of August! 

#EUAIAct #AIgovernance #AImeetup

source_text:
Should we really be worried about AI overtaking humankind? 

In our latest podcast, we talk about the “AI 2027” scenario, which speculates a plausible path to our extinction, driven by artificial general intelligence (AGI). 

Our hosts Evertjan Peer and Cascha van Wanrooij sit down with Paul Kuyer to unpack the paper’s premises and address key questions: 

🔎 Is the “AI 2027” scenario realistic? 
🔎 Are we overestimating AI’s future capabilities? 
🔎 Is superintelligence a mathematical certainty or a promise based on multiple unknowns? 

🎧 Listen to the full discussion here: https://lnkd.in/eFGHnCyE

👇 Do you think we're underestimating or overhyping AGI? 
Share your thoughts in the comments! 

#TheKickstartAIpodcast #AGI #AI2027

AI: a shield or a sword? 

AI is playing a growing role in keeping us safe, from stopping cyber threats to detecting financial crime and improving military readiness. But its power also comes with risks. 

In our latest feature, we explore how AI is used across cybersecurity, fraud detection, threat prediction, and national defense — and how experts in the Netherlands are working to make these systems safer and more responsible. 

We spoke with: 
🔸 Natasha Govender-Ropert, PhD (Rabobank)
🔸 Stjepan Picek (Radboud University)
🔸 Iskander Hannivoort (Manukai)
🔸 Captain van den Broek (Netherlands Royal Army)

📖 Read the full piece below: 
https://lnkd.in/eKpneRZb

source_text:
Last Friday, we met with MPs Barbara Kathmann (PvdA/GL), Martijn Buijsse(VVD), and Jan Valize (PVV) to share what we’re doing at KickstartAI.

Together with our partners — Ahold Delhaize, bol, ING, KLM Royal Dutch Airlines, and Nederlandse Spoorwegen — we highlighted how we’re turning AI into real-world solutions.

From reducing food waste at KLM, to enhancing financial inclusion at ING, improving station safety with NS, and enabling targeted customer experiences at Bol — we jointly focused on concrete, high-impact cases of AI in action.

As Nils Beers, CEO of KickstartAI, said:
“Adoption of AI is not a choice. The opportunity is now if we want to compete. So we need to get to work on actually using AI in our businesses and government.”

We’re encouraged by the support from the MPs and look forward to building momentum for a more positive and actionable AI narrative in the Netherlands.

#AIadoptionNetherlands #AI #AIforgood #AIpolicy

Our second Community Spotlight is live! 🚀 We’re featuring Michael Dubbeldam, Domain Architect at Technolution — and the driving force behind FlowCube, a camera-based sensor that uses deep learning to understand real-time traffic flow across cars, cyclists, and pedestrians. 

Michael shares how a traffic project in Copenhagen sparked his deep dive into vision AI and how that led to creating a system that understands how people move through cities.

From designing Siamese models for person re-identification to adapting systems across global contexts, Michael and his team are turning vision AI into a practical tool for smarter, more inclusive cities.

belief: Conventional risk assessment methods are no longer adequate for understanding modern national threats.
source_quote: Traditional threat analysis treats risks in isolation. But today, threats are deeply intertwined.
inference_reasoning: By contrasting traditional analysis with the current intertwined nature of threats, the text presupposes that existing methods are insufficient for present conditions.
source_text:
Great news! 🚀 We collaborated with DenkWerk on their new report examining how the Netherlands can better prepare for an increasingly interconnected threat landscape.

🔎 The challenge: 
Traditional threat analysis treats risks in isolation. But today, threats are deeply intertwined. A cyberattack can disrupt supply chains. Climate events can ripple into economic shocks. As COVID showed, health crises can spark nationwide consequences.

💡 Our contribution: 
Thanks to the work of our ML Engineer Cascha van Wanrooij, we developed a node analysis tool that maps how threats connect and amplify each other.

We used LLMs to extract causal relationships from thousands of government and research documents — creating a semantic network of interlinked national threats. The result: an interactive graph that helps policymakers explore these complex threat relationships visually, moving beyond traditional "probability × impact" models to include interconnectedness as a crucial third dimension. (Link to our tool: https://lnkd.in/dqej7mDQ)

👉 Read the full report here: https://lnkd.in/d7ABqq7f

#ResilienceByDesign #AIforGood #KickstartAI

belief: Policymakers need tools that represent threats as interconnected systems rather than as separate, independent risks.
source_quote: moving beyond traditional "probability × impact" models to include interconnectedness as a crucial third dimension
inference_reasoning: The call to move beyond traditional models and add interconnectedness implies that effective policymaking requires system-level, network-based representations of risk.

belief: Traditional performance metrics like OKRs and KPIs are structurally misaligned with the kind of organisational change required for effective AI adoption.
source_quote: We must go beyond traditional metrics like OKRs and KPIs. They simply don’t work.
inference_reasoning: By asserting that OKRs and KPIs "don’t work" in this context, the text presupposes that these tools are not just insufficient in practice but fundamentally mismatched to the transformational nature of AI initiatives.
source_text:
Yesterday at TNW Conference, our very own Sophia Claire Zitman, addressed why AI adoption is lagging despite its €85bn potential for the Dutch economy. The problem isn't the tech or talent; it's that AI is too big to fully grasp.

As Sophia said: “AI isn’t digital transformation. It's not about automating things. It is about rewriting, reshaping, and rethinking the way you work.” 

To realize its potential, we must go beyond traditional metrics like OKRs and KPIs. They simply don’t work. The key is finding the entrepreneurial spirits and change-makers within your organizations — and enabling them to break the mould.  

⚙️ In our projects, this approach has led to real results:
🥗 KLM Royal Dutch Airlines cut food waste by 65%
📦 bol improved parcel delivery efficiency by 6%
🚉 Nederlandse Spoorwegen train stations became safer

Let's stop forcing AI into old frameworks — and embrace the change it brings. 

#KickstartAI #TNWConference2025 #AIadoptionNetherlands

belief: AI safety is an important and legitimate topic that warrants serious, structured discussion rather than dismissal or hype.
source_quote: dive into the growing concerns around AI safety
inference_reasoning: Framing AI safety concerns as something to "dive into" and structuring a full episode around them presupposes that these concerns are substantive and deserving of careful examination, not fringe or trivial.
source_text:
Are we worried about the wrong AI apocalypse?

In our latest podcast episode, our hosts Cascha van Wanrooij and Evertjan Peer sit down with philosopher and product architect Paul Kuyer from AxonIQ to dive into the growing concerns around AI safety. From existential risks to immediate dangers, we unpack how (and if) AI could go astray.

We discuss and challenge the “AI 2027” paper, which speculates on a catastrophic future shaped by artificial general intelligence (AGI).

Key topics include:
 🔹 Misalignment: AI’s goals diverging from human values.
 🔹 Misuse: How bad actors could exploit AI for malicious purposes like cyber warfare and manipulation.

We also discuss assumptions around AI’s future capabilities, the differences between the human brain and neural networks, and why we tend to anthropomorphize AI.

🎧 Ready to dive into the debate?
 👉 Listen to full conversation here: https://lnkd.in/eFGHnCyE

The EU AI act is reshaping how we build, deploy, and oversee AI systems — but are you ready for it? 

On July 3rd, join us for a crucial meetup that will help turn complex policy into practical tools. Together with BigData Republic, we’re bringing the AI Act down to earth and into your day-to-day work. 

What to expect: 

🔸 Kaan K. and Cas Mevissen (Policy Officers on AI, Dutch Ministries of Justice and Economic Affairs) will break down the core elements of the AI Act and present the government's rollout plans. 

🔸 Jurriaan Parie (co-founder of Algorithm Audit) will share real-world insights from public audits of machine learning systems. 

🔸 Rens van Dongen (AI Officer at Nederlandse Spoorwegen) will discuss how NS is implementing an internal AI Management System (AIMS) to embed security, ethics, and future-readiness into their AI practice. 

Whether you're an engineer writing code, a strategist defining AI policy, or a compliance professional, this meetup is your essential opportunity to get informed, ask questions, and prepare for what's next.

👉 Interested? Register below!

#EUAIAct #AImeetup #AIinNetherlands

At yesterday’s KickstartAI x distil labs workshop, we rolled up our sleeves to explore how fine-tuning SLMs can unlock powerful and cost-effective RAG systems.

🚀 The result? A packed room, lively discussion, and a hands-on look at how to make small models punch above their weight.

💡 Our takeaway: With the right tools and strategy small models can deliver big results — faster and cheaper.

Special thanks to Jacek Golebiowski and Selim Nowicki from diistil labs for leading us through:

🔹 The full SLM fine-tuning pipeline — from lightweight dataset prep to RAG deployment
🔹 A live demo on adapting compact models with just a few examples
🔹 Case studies showing how this approach cuts costs and dev time without sacrificing performance
🔹 A look at the distil labs platform, which simplifies the development and deployment of specialized models

💬 We’d love to hear your takeaways or follow-up thoughts in the comments!

#KickstartAIcommunity #AIevents #SLMs

belief: Political instability and slow policymaking are inherent constraints on addressing AI-related challenges, so non-governmental actors must drive progress independently of government timelines.

source_text:
The Dutch government has fallen. But AI progress can’t wait.

Political views aside — this government shouldn't have collapsed. Not now. The challenges we face are too big and too urgent. But it’s also a clear signal: we can’t wait for The Hague alone to solve them.

The future won’t be shaped in debate rooms — it will be shaped by action. By people who dare to do.

At KickstartAI, we build real-world AI solutions every day that make the Netherlands smarter, fairer, and more human. We believe leadership in AI will come from those who take action and responsibility.

No more talk. No more delays.

It’s time to:
✅ Accelerate real AI adoption in Dutch businesses and institutions
✅ Show that AI delivers results and social impact

We’re joining forces with those who believe the Netherlands should lead in applied AI — not just talk about it. And in doing so, we aim to give future policymakers something solid to build on: real lessons to take into manifestos, election debates, and a new coalition agreement.

Let’s come together. And make it happen.

Ahold Delhaize KLM Royal Dutch Airlines Nederlandse Spoorwegen ING Nederland Ampère bol

source_text:
The new episode of The KickstartAI Podcast is live 🚀 

In this episode, our hosts Evertjan Peer and Cascha V. sit down with Johan de Vries, Tech Lead Battery Optimization at Dexter Energy. Together, they explore how data and AI are used to manage Europe’s increasingly complex energy grid. 

We cover: 

📊 High-resolution forecasting:
 How Dexter uses weather data to predict renewable energy generation — and why precision matters as volatility grows.
📈Market optimization: 
 From day-ahead to intraday and imbalance markets, we break down how energy is traded and balanced across Europe.
🌍 The changing grid: 
 More renewables, more electric vehicles, and more prosumers mean more complexity — and smarter solutions.
💥 Plus: how France helped mitigate the Spain/Portugal outage — and more! 

This one is a deep dive into the future of energy, data, and AI. 🔌

💬 What do you think is the biggest challenge in managing Europe’s grid? Let us know in the comments!

Listen to full podcast below!

#TheKickstartAIpodcast #AIinEnergy

https://lnkd.in/e-9ZtqRu

Workshop reminder 📢 

Curious about energy-efficient dike construction? Join us this Thursday for a hands-on workshop.

The challenge: 
➡️ Create a conceptual solution that optimizes the logistics of matching soil supply and demand — while keeping energy use to the minimum. 

We’ll be looking at approaches like Multi-Agent Systems (MAS), Reinforcement Learning (RL), and Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP), but we’re excited to hear your own ideas! 

We’re kicking the evening off with a talk by Rohit Gupta, PhD, Data Science and Analytics Leader at KLM Royal Dutch Airlines. 

A big thank you to Boskalis for co-hosting this workshop with us!

belief: Improving the energy efficiency and sustainability of AI infrastructure is an important and legitimate concern for the AI community.
source_quote: AI is transforming the world, but at what cost?
inference_reasoning: By juxtaposing AI’s transformative impact with a cost question and then focusing the entire event on optimizing AI’s energy use, the text presupposes that energy and sustainability concerns are significant and should matter to those working with AI.
source_text:
AI is transforming the world, but at what cost? During our Meetup yesterday in Amsterdam, we explored ways to optimize AI’s energy use. 

David Kohnstamm (founder and CSO at leafcloud), challenged the conventional thinking around efficiency in IT infrastructure, and advocated for alternative cloud models that prioritize effectiveness and sustainability. 

Wilco Burggraaf (Principal Lead at HighTech Innovators) delved into practical methods on how to track and manage AI’s energy footprint — using tools like the Software Carbon Intensity (SCI) formula and built-in energy tracking for AI workloads on Kubernetes.

Robert Keus (founder of GreenPT) and Cas Burggraaf (CTO at GreenPT) shared how they’ve set up a scalable (and green) AI platform. They also stressed a key takeaway: educating users to prompt better is essential to cutting energy consumption. 

Thanks to everyone who joined us yesterday, and we look forward to seeing you at the next Meetup in June! 

#KickstartAI #AIcommunityNetherlands #SustainableAI

source_text:
🧠 How do you identify an AI use case and turn it into a solution that actually works — in the real world, with real people, under real pressure?

In this video, we take you behind the scenes of our project with Erasmus MC, where we are building an AI tool to help predict ER patient inflow, providing staff with better foresight and helping the hospital manage capacity more effectively.

But the real story isn’t just about the model.
It’s about the process:
🔍 Understanding the real operational need
🗣️ Walking the floor and talking to users
🧩 Mapping where AI fits into existing workflows
📍 Designing for adoption from day one

This is how we believe applied AI should be done — designed for real needs, shaped by people, and built for impact.

A big thank you to Sophia Claire Zitman, Margrietha H. (Greet) Vink, Marjolein Kremers, Nicky Hekster for their insights!

Watch the full breakdown of practical steps and advice in the video below 👇 

#KickstartAI #ErasmusMC #AIimplementation

Is forecasting time series data easier than ever? 

In their latest blogpost on Medium, our data scientist Dr. Carmen Adriana Martínez Barbosa and Jose Celis-Gil, data scientist at Odido, explore the capabilities of universal forecasting, moving beyond the “one model per dataset” premise. 

They experiment with Moirae-MoE — a Large Time Series Model — and evaluate its strengths and limitations. 

Curious about how Moirae-MoE performs? Read the full blog below 👇

source_text:
⏰ Three days to go for our next meetup ⏰ 

How do we deploy AI systems that optimize energy use, while reducing their own environmental impact? Join us for a deep technical dive in Amsterdam! 

We’re bringing together experts who are tackling this challenge head-on! 

David Kohnstamm will bring you a fresh take on why the push for efficiency in AI infrastructure might be backfiring (and what we should focus on instead). 

Wilco Burggraaf will guide you through practical methods for tracking AI’s energy footprint, using real-time CO₂ monitoring, the SCI formula, and Kubernetes-based energy tracking.

Robert Keus and Cas Burggraaf will share their practical approach to building a scalable AI platform. They’ll explore practical architectural choices, focusing on containerization to ensure your AI infrastructure can scale as your needs grow. 

See you there!

We took Nvidia’s Cosmos model for a spin and created a realistic driving simulation in Amsterdam 🚘 

Advancements in machine learning show that even relatively small models (like the 7B-parameter Cosmos) can generate diverse, physically plausible synthetic data, scenarios, and even entire worlds on demand.

This capability enables developers to test AI systems across billions of virtual kilometers and countless scenarios — leading to safer, more capable AI for real-world applications like driving.

What's your take on this? Drop a comment and join the discussion 👇

source_text:
🗣️ Applications for this year’s National AI Challenge are open!

Together with AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL, we’re calling on organizations looking to use AI to solve some of the world’s most pressing societal challenges. This is your chance to create solutions that drive real change and improve people’s lives!

Following a successful 2024 edition, this year, we’ll select two winners and work with them on two separate projects.

💡 Who can apply? 
Private, public, or social organisations that are (or interested in becoming) members of AIC4NL.

Selection criteria: 
➡️ Relevance and urgency of the problem
➡️ Available support by your organisation
➡️ Feasibility
➡️ Impact and scalability
➡️ Willingness to share the results

Why join? 
✅ Develop a working AI solution for your organization together with experts
✅ Make social impact 
✅ Gain access to technical expertise, project support, and a powerful network

source_text:
🗣️ Applications for this year’s National AI Challenge are open!

Together with AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL, we’re calling on organizations looking to use AI to solve some of the world’s most pressing societal challenges. This is your chance to create solutions that drive real change and improve people’s lives!

Following a successful 2024 edition, this year, we’ll select two winners and work with them on two separate projects.

💡 Who can apply? 
Private, public, or social organisations that are (or interested in becoming) members of AIC4NL.

Selection criteria: 
➡️ Relevance and urgency of the problem
➡️ Available support by your organisation
➡️ Feasibility
➡️ Impact and scalability
➡️ Willingness to share the results

Why join? 
✅ Develop a working AI solution for your organization together with experts
✅ Make social impact

belief: Successful application of AI in healthcare operations depends more on careful problem definition, feasibility assessment, and integration planning than on the AI technology itself.
source_quote: what it really takes to apply AI in hospital operational processes
inference_reasoning: By emphasizing understanding the problem, assessing feasibility, and determining integration and adoption processes as the core lessons, the text implies that these upstream and organizational factors are the true determinants of success rather than the mere presence of AI tools.
source_text:
At the Dutch AI Congress this week, we had the opportunity to share key insights on how to develop and implement AI solutions in healthcare organizations.

For the past seven months, we’ve been working closely with Erasmus MC to predict patient inflow at the emergency department — a step toward improving efficiency, capacity management, and staffing.

Our Tech Lead, Evertjan P., took the stage to talk about what it really takes to apply AI in hospital operational processes. Here are three of the most important lessons we've learned so far:

💡 Thoroughly understand and define the problem you’re aiming to solve:
This is key to creating a specific goal and a concrete plan.

💡 Assess feasibility: 
What data sources are available, and what level of performance can an AI model achieve based on these? How can we use these AI-driven insights in day-to-day practice?

💡 Determine the processes for tech integration and user adoption:
Ask: What’s the IT infrastructure like? How will the AI tool be presented to end-users? How should it be used in practice?

We’re proud to be part of the AI movement in the Netherlands — and excited for what’s next. 🧡 

A big shoutout to the AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL for organizing this inspiring congress!

#AI #HealthcareAI #HealthcareInnovation

source_text:
Very proud to announce that we once again joined forces with AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL to accelerate AI adoption in the Netherlands. Frans Muller, CEO of Ahold Delhaize, one of the founding partner companies of KickstartAI, today unveiled the second edition of the National AI Challenge, the flagship initiative of our renewed collaboration. 

The National AI Challenge was first introduced by KickstartAI and AI Coalition 4 NL in 2024, aiming to solve  concrete challenges in businesses and social organizations with artificial intelligence. The winner of the first edition, Erasmus MC, has worked with KickstartAI over the past year to improve efficiency and quality of care in the Emergency Department using AI technology.

This year’s National AI Challenge will start at the end of April and will select not one, but two use cases.

Read more about our collaboration in our press release https://lnkd.in/d-MnC-Vc  and stay tuned for more details about how to participate in the Challenge!

Pieter Jeekel Nils Beers Tjarda Voorneman Frans Muller Keely Richardson Klaas Wagenaar
